title: Morning Tea Shop
text_zh: 早上六点，一个小茶店开门了。一个老人坐在里面喝茶，看书。他每天都来。店里很安静，只有他一个人。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang liù diǎn, yí gè xiǎo chádiàn kāi mén le. Yí gè lǎorén zuò zài lǐmiàn hē chá, kàn shū. Tā měi tiān dōu lái. Diàn lǐ hěn ānjìng, zhǐ yǒu tā yí gè rén.
text_en: At six in the morning, a small tea shop opens. An old man sits inside drinking tea and reading. He comes every day. The shop is quiet — he's the only one there.

questions.q_zh: 为什么店里只有老人一个人？
questions.q_en: Why is the old man the only one in the shop?

options.text_en: because the shop is too small

questions.explanation: He arrives at six in the morning — no other customers come that early.

questions.q_zh: 老人来得很早说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the old man arriving so early suggest about him?

options.pinyin: hē chá shì tā měi tiān de xíguàn
options.text_en: drinking tea is his daily routine
he lives next to the tea shop

questions.explanation: Notice 坐在里面喝茶, which suggests he comes every day to drink tea and read — it's his fixed daily habit.

title: Rain on the Window
text_zh: 今天下雨了。我坐在家里看窗外面。雨不大，但是很好看。街上没有人。一只猫坐在商店前面。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xià yǔ le. Wǒ zuò zài jiā lǐ kàn chuāng wàimiàn. Yǔ bú dà, dànshì hěn hǎokàn. Jiē shàng méiyǒu rén. Yì zhī māo zuò zài shāngdiàn qiánmiàn.
text_en: It's raining today. I sit at home looking out the window. The rain isn't heavy, but it's beautiful. There's nobody on the street. A cat sits in front of the shop.

questions.q_en: Are there people on the street?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有人 (nobody) in the passage — there's nobody on the street.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: The narrator finds beauty in light rain and a quiet street — noticing the charm of an ordinary moment.

title: The Old Man in the Park
text_zh: 每天早上，公园里有很多老人。有的人在走，有的人坐着看别人。一个老人每天都带一杯茶来，坐在同一个地方。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang, gōngyuán lǐ yǒu hěn duō lǎorén. Yǒude rén zài zǒu, yǒude rén zuòzhe kàn biéren. Yí gè lǎorén měi tiān dōu dài yì bēi chá lái, zuò zài tóng yí gè dìfang.
text_en: Every morning, there are many old people in the park. Some walk, some sit and watch others. One old man brings a cup of tea every day and sits in the same spot.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: He brings tea and sits in the same spot every single day, showing strong consistency and determination.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: Old people walking, sitting, watching others, and drinking tea in the park paints a calm, peaceful picture.

title: The Bicycle Under the Tree
text_zh: 学校前面有一棵大树。树下有很多自行车。每天下午，学生们来拿自行车回家。但是有一辆自行车，没有人来拿。它在那里很多天了。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào qiánmiàn yǒu yì kē dà shù. Shù xià yǒu hěn duō zìxíngchē. Měi tiān xiàwǔ, xuéshēngmen lái ná zìxíngchē huí jiā. Dànshì yǒu yí liàng zìxíngchē, méiyǒu rén lái ná. Tā zài nàlǐ hěn duō tiān le.
text_en: In front of the school there's a big tree. Under the tree are many bicycles. Every afternoon, students come to get their bikes and go home. But one bicycle — nobody comes for it. It's been there for many days.

questions.q_en: What happened with that bicycle?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有人来拿 (nobody comes for it) in the passage — one bicycle has been there for many days — nobody comes to get.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景最让人印象深刻的是什么？
questions.q_en: What is most memorable about this scene?
questions.explanation: The forgotten bicycle creates a lingering mood — it's the feeling, not the facts, that stays with you.

title: The Light in the Window
text_zh: 晚上，对面的楼有很多灯。有的亮，有的不亮。我喜欢看那些灯。十一点以后，灯一个一个地关了。最后只有一个灯还亮着。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang, duìmiàn de lóu yǒu hěn duō dēng. Yǒude liàng, yǒude bú liàng. Wǒ xǐhuan kàn nàxiē dēng. Shíyī diǎn yǐhòu, dēng yí gè yí gè de guān le. Zuìhòu zhǐ yǒu yí gè dēng hái liàngzhe.
text_en: At night, the building across the way has many lights. Some are on, some are off. I like watching those lights. After eleven, the lights go off one by one. In the end, only one light stays on.

questions.q_en: What happens after eleven?

options.pinyin: dēng yí gè yí gè guān le
options.text_en: lights go off one by one

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 灯一个一个地 — after eleven, the lights go off one by one until only one remains.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: The narrator enjoys simply watching lights from a window — finding contentment in a quiet, simple moment.

title: The Street Sweeper
text_zh: 每天早上五点半，有一个人在我家前面扫地。他穿很旧的衣服。天冷的时候，他也来。天热的时候，他也来。我不认识他，但是我每天都能听到他。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang wǔ diǎn bàn, yǒu yí gè rén zài wǒ jiā qiánmiàn sǎo dì. Tā chuān hěn jiù de yīfu. Tiān lěng de shíhou, tā yě lái. Tiān rè de shíhou, tā yě lái. Wǒ bú rènshi tā, dànshì wǒ měi tiān dōu néng tīngdào tā.
text_en: Every morning at five thirty, someone sweeps in front of my home. He wears old clothes. When it's cold, he still comes. When it's hot, he still comes. I don't know him, but I can hear him every day.

questions.q_en: What time does he come to sweep?
questions.explanation: Look for 五点半 (five thirty) in the opening line — he comes every morning at five thirty.

questions.q_en: Does he come when it's cold?
questions.explanation: Look for 来 (comes) in the third sentence — when it's cold he still comes, when it's hot he still comes — he always.

title: Spring Comes to the Alley
text_zh: 今天天气很好，不冷了。小街上的树有新的叶子了。一个老太太在门前面坐着。她的猫也在。春天来了。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān tiānqì hěn hǎo, bù lěng le. Xiǎo jiē shàng de shù yǒu xīn de yèzi le. Yí gè lǎo tàitai zài mén qiánmiàn zuòzhe. Tā de māo yě zài. Chūntiān lái le.
text_en: The weather is nice today, it's not cold anymore. The trees on the little street have new leaves. An old lady sits in front of her door. Her cat is there too. Spring has come.

questions.q_en: How's the weather today?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 很好 — the weather is nice today, not cold anymore, and spring has come.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The old lady sits calmly with her cat as spring arrives — nothing suggests change, just steady peace.

title: Watching the Rain Stop
text_zh: 下午三点开始下雨。我在一个小商店里面等。商店的人给我一杯热水。我们没有说话，一起看外面的雨。四点的时候，雨停了。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sān diǎn kāishǐ xià yǔ. Wǒ zài yí gè xiǎo shāngdiàn lǐmiàn děng. Shāngdiàn de rén gěi wǒ yì bēi rè shuǐ. Wǒmen méiyǒu shuō huà, yìqǐ kàn wàimiàn de yǔ. Sì diǎn de shíhou, yǔ tíng le.
text_en: At three in the afternoon it starts raining. I wait inside a small shop. The shop person gives me a cup of hot water. We don't talk — we watch the rain outside together. At four, the rain stops.

questions.q_en: What did the shop person give?
questions.explanation: Look for 一杯热水 (a cup of hot water) in the third sentence — the shop person gives a cup of hot water.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: Strangers sharing silence while watching rain together creates a quiet, gentle scene.

title: The Sound Upstairs
text_zh: 每天晚上九点，楼上有人在走。走得很慢。然后有水的声音。然后安静了。每天都一样。我不知道楼上住的是谁。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān wǎnshang jiǔ diǎn, lóu shàng yǒu rén zài zǒu. Zǒu de hěn màn. Ránhòu yǒu shuǐ de shēngyīn. Ránhòu ānjìng le. Měi tiān dōu yíyàng. Wǒ bù zhīdào lóu shàng zhù de shì shéi.
text_en: Every night at nine, someone walks upstairs. Walking very slowly. Then the sound of water. Then it's quiet. The same every day. I don't know who lives upstairs.

questions.q_en: How does the person upstairs walk?
questions.explanation: Look for 很慢 (very slowly) in the second sentence — the person upstairs walks very slowly.

questions.q_en: Does he know the person upstairs?
questions.explanation: The last sentence says directly: I don't know who lives upstairs.

title: The Same Bus
text_zh: 我每天坐同一辆公共汽车去工作。车上有一个女人，她也每天坐这辆车。我们不说话，但是我们都知道。今天她没有来。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān zuò tóng yí liàng gōnggòng qìchē qù gōngzuò. Chē shàng yǒu yí gè nǚrén, tā yě měi tiān zuò zhè liàng chē. Wǒmen bù shuō huà, dànshì wǒmen dōu zhīdào. Jīntiān tā méiyǒu lái.
text_en: I take the same bus to work every day. There's a woman on the bus — she takes this bus every day too. We don't talk, but we both know. Today she didn't come.

questions.q_en: Do they talk?
questions.explanation: Look for 我们不说话， — it directly confirms don't talk.

questions.q_en: What happened today?
questions.explanation: The last sentence says today she didn't come.

title: The Old Street
text_zh: 这条街很老了。两边有很多小商店。有的商店卖水果，有的卖书。街不大，但是很多人喜欢来这里走走。
text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo jiē hěn lǎo le. Liǎng biān yǒu hěn duō xiǎo shāngdiàn. Yǒude shāngdiàn mài shuǐguǒ, yǒude mài shū. Jiē bú dà, dànshì hěn duō rén xǐhuan lái zhèlǐ zǒuzou.
text_en: This street is old. On both sides there are many small shops. Some shops sell fruit, some sell books. The street isn't big, but many people like to come here for a walk.

questions.q_en: What do the shops sell?
questions.explanation: Look for 商店卖水果 (fruit and books) in the third sentence — some shops sell fruit, some sell books.

questions.q_en: Is this street big?
questions.explanation: Look for 不大 (not big) in the final sentence — the street isn't big.

title: The Building Being Built
text_zh: 我家后面在建一个新的大楼。每天早上有很多工人。他们工作的声音很大。以前那里是一个小公园。现在公园没有了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā hòumiàn zài jiàn yí gè xīn de dà lóu. Měi tiān zǎoshang yǒu hěn duō gōngrén. Tāmen gōngzuò de shēngyīn hěn dà. Yǐqián nàlǐ shì yí gè xiǎo gōngyuán. Xiànzài gōngyuán méiyǒu le.
text_en: Behind my home they're building a new tall building. Every morning there are many workers. The sound of their work is loud. Before, there was a small park there. Now the park is gone.

questions.q_en: What was there before?
questions.explanation: Look for 一个小公园 (a small park) in the passage — before, there was a small park there.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The construction is ongoing but routine — the tone suggests daily life just carries on as usual.

title: The Corner Shop
text_zh: 街角有一个很小的商店。里面什么都有：水、茶、书、水果。店里只有一个人在工作，是一个老先生。他认识这条街上的每一个人。
text_pinyin: Jiējiǎo yǒu yí gè hěn xiǎo de shāngdiàn. Lǐmiàn shénme dōu yǒu: shuǐ, chá, shū, shuǐguǒ. Diàn lǐ zhǐ yǒu yí gè rén zài gōngzuò, shì yí gè lǎo xiānsheng. Tā rènshi zhè tiáo jiē shàng de měi yí gè rén.
text_en: On the street corner there's a small shop. It has everything inside: water, tea, books, fruit. Only one person works in the shop — an old gentleman. He knows every person on this street.

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: He runs a small shop alone and knows everyone on the street — that's a deliberate lifestyle choice.

questions.q_en: Who does the old gentleman know?

options.pinyin: jiē shàng de měi gè rén

questions.explanation: Look for 认识这条街上 (everyone on the street) in the final sentence — he knows every person on this street.

title: Two Doors
text_zh: 我住的楼有两个门。前面的门很大，很漂亮。后面的门很小，不好看。但是大家都走后面的门，因为前面的门不能开。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de lóu yǒu liǎng gè mén. Qiánmiàn de mén hěn dà, hěn piàoliang. Hòumiàn de mén hěn xiǎo, bù hǎokàn. Dànshì dàjiā dōu zǒu hòumiàn de mén, yīnwèi qiánmiàn de mén bù néng kāi.
text_en: The building I live in has two doors. The front door is big and beautiful. The back door is small and plain. But everyone uses the back door, because the front door can't be opened.

questions.q_zh: 美的描写在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the description of beauty play?
questions.explanation: Describing the front door as big and beautiful creates a feeling — it sets up the irony of it being unusable.

questions.q_en: What's the front door like?
questions.explanation: Look for 很漂亮 (big, beautiful) in the second sentence — the front door is big and beautiful.

title: The Roof
text_zh: 我住在六楼。从我的窗户能看到很多楼的上面。天好的时候，有人在楼上晒衣服。有时候我能看到一个人在上面坐着看天。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài liù lóu. Cóng wǒ de chuānghu néng kàndào hěn duō lóu de shàngmiàn. Tiān hǎo de shíhou, yǒu rén zài lóu shàng shài yīfu. Yǒu shíhou wǒ néng kàndào yí gè rén zài shàngmiàn zuòzhe kàn tiān.
text_en: I live on the sixth floor. From my window I can see the tops of many buildings. When the weather is nice, people hang clothes to dry up there. Sometimes I can see someone sitting up there looking at the sky.

questions.q_en: What floor does he live on?
questions.explanation: Look for 六楼 (sixth floor) in the opening line — I live on the sixth floor.

questions.q_zh: 天好的时候楼上有什么？
questions.q_en: What's on the rooftops when the weather is nice?
questions.explanation: Look for 晒的衣服 (clothes drying) — when the weather is nice, people hang clothes to dry on the rooftops.

title: The Library Morning
text_zh: 图书馆每天早上九点开门。门还没开的时候，外面已经有人在等了。第一个进去的总是一个老人。他每天都看同一本书。
text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn měi tiān zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn kāi mén. Mén hái méi kāi de shíhou, wàimiàn yǐjīng yǒu rén zài děng le. Dì yī gè jìnqù de zǒng shì yí gè lǎorén. Tā měi tiān dōu kàn tóng yì běn shū.
text_en: The library opens at nine every morning. Before the door opens, people are already waiting outside. The first one in is always an old man. He reads the same book every day.

questions.q_en: What time does the library open?
questions.explanation: Look for 图书馆每天早 (nine) in the opening line — the library opens at nine every morning.

questions.q_en: What book does the old man read?
questions.explanation: Look for 每天都看同 (the same book) in the final sentence — he reads the same book every day.

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 医院里有很多人在等。有的人在看手机，有的人在看窗外面。一个小孩在妈妈的腿上睡觉。这里很安静，没有人说话。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn lǐ yǒu hěn duō rén zài děng. Yǒude rén zài kàn shǒujī, yǒude rén zài kàn chuāng wàimiàn. Yí gè xiǎohái zài māma de tuǐ shàng shuìjiào. Zhèlǐ hěn ānjìng, méiyǒu rén shuō huà.
text_en: Many people are waiting in the hospital. Some look at their phones, some look out the window. A small child sleeps on her mother's lap. It's quiet here — nobody talks.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: Describing phones, windows, a sleeping child — these details put the reader inside the waiting room.

questions.q_en: Is it quiet in the hospital?
questions.explanation: Look for 很安静 (very quiet) in the closing line — it's quiet here, nobody talks.

title: Who Moved My Cup?
text_zh: 今天早上我的杯子不在桌子上了。昨天晚上它在那里。我问爸爸，爸爸说不知道。我问妈妈，妈妈笑了。杯子在厨房里。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān zǎoshang wǒ de bēizi bú zài zhuōzi shàng le. Zuótiān wǎnshang tā zài nàlǐ. Wǒ wèn bàba, bàba shuō bù zhīdào. Wǒ wèn māma, māma xiào le. Bēizi zài chúfáng lǐ.
text_en: This morning my cup wasn't on the table anymore. Last night it was there. I asked dad — he said he didn't know. I asked mom — she laughed. The cup was in the kitchen.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The real interest is the narrator's curiosity and mom's knowing laugh — the feelings behind the event.

questions.q_en: Who knows where the cup is?
questions.explanation: Mom laughed when asked, meaning she knew where the cup was all along.

title: The Note on the Door
text_zh: 今天回家的时候，门上有一张纸条。上面写着：明天下午三点来。但是没有名字。谁写的？我不知道。明天三点，也许我会在家。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān huí jiā de shíhou, mén shàng yǒu yì zhāng zhǐtiáo. Shàngmiàn xiězhe: míngtiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn lái. Dànshì méiyǒu míngzi. Shéi xiě de? Wǒ bù zhīdào. Míngtiān sān diǎn, yěxǔ wǒ huì zài jiā.
text_en: When I came home today, there was a note on the door. It said: come tomorrow at three PM. But there was no name. Who wrote it? I don't know. Tomorrow at three, maybe I'll be home.

questions.q_en: What did the note say?
questions.explanation: The note says come tomorrow at three PM.

questions.q_en: Was there a name on the note?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有 (no) in the third sentence — there was no name on the note.

title: First Day in a New City
text_zh: 我昨天到了一个新的城市。这里的人说话很快，我听不懂。商店很多，但是我不知道去哪个。我走了很多路。晚上回到家，很累，但是很开心。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zuótiān dào le yí gè xīn de chéngshì. Zhèlǐ de rén shuō huà hěn kuài, wǒ tīng bù dǒng. Shāngdiàn hěn duō, dànshì wǒ bù zhīdào qù nǎge. Wǒ zǒu le hěn duō lù. Wǎnshang huí dào jiā, hěn lèi, dànshì hěn kāixīn.
text_en: I arrived in a new city yesterday. People here talk fast — I can't understand. There are many shops, but I don't know which one to go to. I walked a lot. In the evening I got home, tired but happy.

questions.q_zh: 他能听懂这里的人说话吗？
questions.q_en: Can he understand the people here?
questions.explanation: Look for 人说话很快， (can't understand) in the second sentence — people here talk fast and he can't understand.

questions.q_en: How does he feel in the evening?
questions.explanation: The clue is 累但是开心 (tired but happy) — in the evening he got home, tired but happy.

title: Tea with the Neighbor
text_zh: 下午的时候，邻居来我家喝茶。她带了一些水果。我们坐在桌子前面，一边喝茶一边说话。她告诉我这里什么商店好。时间过得很快。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ de shíhou, línjū lái wǒ jiā hē chá. Tā dài le yìxiē shuǐguǒ. Wǒmen zuò zài zhuōzi qiánmiàn, yìbiān hē chá yìbiān shuō huà. Tā gàosu wǒ zhèlǐ shénme shāngdiàn hǎo. Shíjiān guò de hěn kuài.
text_en: In the afternoon, my neighbor comes to my home for tea. She brought some fruit. We sit at the table, drinking tea and talking. She tells me which shops are good around here. Time passes quickly.

questions.q_en: What did the neighbor bring?
questions.explanation: Look for 水果 (fruit) in the second sentence — she brought some fruit.

questions.q_en: What did she tell him?
questions.explanation: Look for 什么商店好 (which shops are good) in the passage — she tells him which shops are good around here.

title: At the Train Station
text_zh: 火车站很大，人很多。我不知道去哪里买票。我问了一个人，他说在前面左边。我去了，但是那里是厕所。我又问了一个人。这次对了。
text_pinyin: Huǒchēzhàn hěn dà, rén hěn duō. Wǒ bù zhīdào qù nǎlǐ mǎi piào. Wǒ wèn le yí gè rén, tā shuō zài qiánmiàn zuǒbiān. Wǒ qù le, dànshì nàlǐ shì cèsuǒ. Wǒ yòu wèn le yí gè rén. Zhè cì duì le.
text_en: The train station is big, with many people. I don't know where to buy a ticket. I ask someone — he says it's ahead on the left. I go there, but it's the bathroom. I ask another person. This time it's right.

questions.q_zh: 第一次他去的地方是什么？
questions.q_en: What was the first place he went to?
questions.explanation: The first person pointed him to the wrong place — it turned out to be the bathroom.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: He found the ticket office, got on a train — ordinary life just continues from here.

title: Why the Shop Closes Early
text_zh: 我家下面的商店每天下午四点就关门了。我问老板为什么。他说：因为晚上没有人来买东西。他说以前不是这样的，以前晚上人很多。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā xiàmiàn de shāngdiàn měi tiān xiàwǔ sì diǎn jiù guān mén le. Wǒ wèn lǎobǎn wèishénme. Tā shuō: yīnwèi wǎnshang méiyǒu rén lái mǎi dōngxi. Tā shuō yǐqián bú shì zhèyàng de, yǐqián wǎnshang rén hěn duō.
text_en: The shop below my home closes at four PM every day. I ask the owner why. He says: because nobody comes to buy things at night. He says it wasn't like this before — there used to be many people at night.

questions.q_en: What time does the shop close?
questions.explanation: Look for 商店每天下 (four) in the opening line — the shop closes at four PM every day.

questions.q_en: What was it like in the evenings before?
questions.explanation: The owner says it wasn't like this before — there used to be many people at night.

title: Two Cups of Tea
text_zh: 下午三点，我给自己做了两杯茶。一杯在桌子上，一杯在手里。我喝手里的，看桌子上的。两杯茶都不说话。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sān diǎn, wǒ gěi zìjǐ zuò le liǎng bēi chá. Yì bēi zài zhuōzi shàng, yì bēi zài shǒu lǐ. Wǒ hē shǒu lǐ de, kàn zhuōzi shàng de. Liǎng bēi chá dōu bù shuōhuà.
text_en: At three in the afternoon, I make myself two cups of tea. One is on the table, one is in my hand. I drink the one in my hand and watch the one on the table. Neither cup of tea speaks.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: Making two cups and watching one as if it might speak — these details draw you into the narrator's mood.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: Talking to a cup of tea as if it's company — that's the odd, interesting twist in an ordinary moment.

questions.q_en: What time is it?
questions.explanation: Look for 下午三点 (Three in the afternoon) in the opening line — at three in the afternoon.

title: The Dog That Sits
text_zh: 学校前面有一只狗。它每天都来，坐在那里看人。学生来了，它看。学生走了，它也看。没有人叫它来，但是它想来。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào qiánmiàn yǒu yì zhī gǒu. Tā měi tiān dōu lái, zuò zài nàlǐ kàn rén. Xuéshēng lái le, tā kàn. Xuéshēng zǒu le, tā yě kàn. Méiyǒu rén jiào tā lái, dànshì tā xiǎng lái.
text_en: There's a dog in front of the school. It comes every day and sits there watching people. Students arrive — it watches. Students leave — it watches too. Nobody calls it to come, but it wants to come.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这只狗为什么想来学校？
questions.q_en: Why do you think the dog wants to come to the school?
questions.explanation: Nobody calls the dog, but it comes every day to watch people — it simply enjoys watching.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: The dog comes every single day without being called — that's steady, unwavering commitment.

questions.q_en: Who called the dog to come?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有人 (Nobody) in the final sentence — nobody calls it to come.

title: Quiet Afternoon
text_zh: 下午，家里没有人。我一个人坐着喝水。外面有雨，不大。猫在我的书上面睡觉。我不想叫它起来。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ, jiā lǐ méiyǒu rén. Wǒ yí gè rén zuòzhe hē shuǐ. Wàimiàn yǒu yǔ, bú dà. Māo zài wǒ de shū shàngmiàn shuìjiào. Wǒ bù xiǎng jiào tā qǐlái.
text_en: In the afternoon, nobody is home. I sit alone drinking water. There's rain outside, not heavy. The cat is sleeping on top of my book. I don't want to wake it up.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The scene is about the narrator's quiet contentment — mood and thoughts matter more than events.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: Light rain, a sleeping cat, sitting alone with water — these small ordinary moments are presented as valuable.

questions.q_en: What's happening outside?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 下午 — there's rain outside.

title: What the Cat Sees
text_zh: 我的猫坐在那里看外面。我也坐下来看。外面有什么？我看到人在走。猫看到什么？我不知道。可能猫看到的和我不一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de māo zuò zài nàlǐ kàn wàimiàn. Wǒ yě zuò xiàlái kàn. Wàimiàn yǒu shénme? Wǒ kàn dào rén zài zǒu. Māo kàn dào shénme? Wǒ bù zhīdào. Kěnéng māo kàn dào de hé wǒ bù yíyàng.
text_en: My cat sits there looking outside. I sit down and look too. What's outside? I see people walking. What does the cat see? I don't know. Maybe the cat sees something different from me.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: Sitting down to look with the cat gave the narrator a new perspective — wondering what the cat sees differently.

questions.q_en: What do I see outside?
questions.explanation: Look for 人在走 (People walking) in the middle of the passage — I see people walking outside.

questions.q_en: Do I know what the cat sees?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 我不知道 — I don't know what the cat sees — maybe something different.

title: Before Anyone Wakes Up
text_zh: 早上五点，我起床了。家里的人都在睡觉。我一个人坐在那里喝茶。外面没有人，没有车。这个时候，我最高兴。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang wǔ diǎn, wǒ qǐchuáng le. Jiā lǐ de rén dōu zài shuìjiào. Wǒ yí gè rén zuò zài nàlǐ hē chá. Wàimiàn méiyǒu rén, méiyǒu chē. Zhège shíhou, wǒ zuì gāoxìng.
text_en: At five in the morning, I get up. Everyone at home is sleeping. I sit there alone drinking tea. Outside, there are no people, no cars. This is when I'm happiest.

questions.q_en: What time do I wake up?
questions.explanation: Look for 五点 (Five o'clock) in the opening line — at five in the morning, I get up.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The narrator shares personal feelings about quiet mornings — revealing an inner emotional life.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: Drinking tea alone at 5am with no people or cars, and calling it the happiest time — simplicity brings joy.

title: Watching the Rain Stop
text_zh: 下雨了。我在饭店里坐着，喝咖啡，看外面。雨很大，人都在跑。一个小时后，雨小了。人走了，我也走了。但是我不想走。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ le. Wǒ zài fàndiàn lǐ zuòzhe, hē kāfēi, kàn wàimiàn. Yǔ hěn dà, rén dōu zài pǎo. Yí gè xiǎoshí hòu, yǔ xiǎo le. Rén zǒu le, wǒ yě zǒu le. Dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng zǒu.
text_en: It's raining. I sit in a restaurant, drinking coffee, watching outside. The rain is heavy — people are all running. An hour later, the rain lessens. People leave, and I leave too. But I don't want to leave.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: The passage shows the character through actions: sitting, drinking coffee, watching rain, not wanting to leave.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The key is that the narrator doesn't want to leave — the inner reluctance matters more than the rain stopping.

questions.q_en: Do I want to leave?
questions.explanation: The last sentence says I don't want to leave.

title: The Shop That Never Opens
text_zh: 我家前面有一个小商店。但是这个商店不开门。我每天都看，它都关着。里面有很多东西，但是没有人。我问朋友，朋友也不知道为什么。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā qiánmiàn yǒu yí gè xiǎo shāngdiàn. Dànshì zhège shāngdiàn bù kāi mén. Wǒ měi tiān dōu kàn, tā dōu guānzhe. Lǐmiàn yǒu hěn duō dōngxi, dànshì méiyǒu rén. Wǒ wèn péngyou, péngyou yě bù zhīdào wèishénme.
text_en: There's a small shop in front of my home. But this shop doesn't open. I look every day — it's always closed. There are many things inside, but no people. I ask my friend, but my friend doesn't know why either.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: A shop that never opens despite having things inside — that's a strange detail in an otherwise normal street.

questions.q_en: What's inside the shop?
questions.explanation: Look for 很多东西 (Many things) in the passage — there are many things inside.

questions.q_zh: 朋友知道商店为什么不开吗？
questions.q_en: Does the friend know why the shop doesn't open?
questions.explanation: The clue is 不知道 (No) in the closing line — the friend doesn't know why either.

title: Night Bus
text_zh: 晚上十一点，车上的人很少。我坐在后面，看前面的人。一个人在看书，一个人在睡觉。车很大，人很少。我很高兴。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shíyī diǎn, chē shàng de rén hěn shǎo. Wǒ zuò zài hòumiàn, kàn qiánmiàn de rén. Yí gè rén zài kàn shū, yí gè rén zài shuìjiào. Chē hěn dà, rén hěn shǎo. Wǒ hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: At eleven at night, there are few people on the bus. I sit in the back and watch the people in front. One person is reading, one person is sleeping. The bus is big, the people are few. I'm happy.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: Late-night bus, few people, someone reading, someone sleeping — the scene feels calm and tranquil.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: The narrator is happy sitting alone on a nearly empty bus at night — finding peace in simplicity.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: Few people, reading, sleeping, narrator watching quietly — the atmosphere is hushed and contemplative.

title: The Same Road Every Day
text_zh: 我每天走同一条路去工作。路上有一个商店，一个饭店，一个学校。我都认识它们了。今天，商店前面多了一只猫。这让我很高兴。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān zǒu tóng yì tiáo lù qù gōngzuò. Lù shàng yǒu yí gè shāngdiàn, yí gè fàndiàn, yí gè xuéxiào. Wǒ dōu rènshi tāmen le. Jīntiān, shāngdiàn qiánmiàn duō le yì zhī māo. Zhè ràng wǒ hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: I walk the same road to work every day. On the road there's a shop, a restaurant, a school. I know them all now. Today, there's a new cat in front of the shop. This makes me happy.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: Listing the shop, restaurant, school, and new cat creates a vivid picture of the daily walk.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: The passage describes the character's routine and what they notice — their actions reveal who they are.

questions.q_en: What's different today?
questions.explanation: Look for 多了一只猫 (There's a new cat) in the passage — today there's a new cat in front of the shop.

title: Sounds of Home
text_zh: 晚上，我回到家。上面的人在说话，下面的人在听音乐。我在中间。我听到上面的人，也听到下面的人。他们不知道我在听。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang, wǒ huí dào jiā. Shàngmiàn de rén zài shuōhuà, xiàmiàn de rén zài tīng yīnyuè. Wǒ zài zhōngjiān. Wǒ tīng dào shàngmiàn de rén, yě tīng dào xiàmiàn de rén. Tāmen bù zhīdào wǒ zài tīng.
text_en: At night, I return home. The people above are talking, the people below are listening to music. I'm in the middle. I hear the people above and the people below. They don't know I'm listening.

questions.q_zh: 对话中没有说出来的是什么？
questions.q_en: What is left unsaid in the conversation?
questions.explanation: The narrator hears neighbors but says nothing — there are unspoken feelings about being sandwiched between lives.

questions.q_zh: 说话的方式反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the way of speaking reflect?

options.pinyin: shuōhuà rén de xìnggé hé tàidu

questions.explanation: Listening silently while others talk and play music — the narrator's quiet observing reveals their personality.

questions.q_en: Do they know I'm listening?
questions.explanation: Look for 不知道 (No) in the closing line — they don't know I'm listening.

title: Morning at the Train Station
text_zh: 早上七点，火车站里有很多人。有的人在跑，有的人坐着看电话。一个小孩在睡觉，他的妈妈看着他。大家都要去一个地方，但是现在，大家都在这里。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang qī diǎn, huǒchēzhàn lǐ yǒu hěn duō rén. Yǒude rén zài pǎo, yǒude rén zuòzhe kàn diànhuà. Yí gè xiǎohái zài shuìjiào, tā de māma kànzhe tā. Dàjiā dōu yào qù yí gè dìfang, dànshì xiànzài, dàjiā dōu zài zhèlǐ.
text_en: At seven in the morning, the train station is full of people. Some run, some sit looking at their phones. A small child is sleeping — his mother watches him. Everyone wants to go somewhere, but right now, everyone is here.

questions.q_en: What place is this?
questions.explanation: Look for 火车站 (Train station) in the opening line — it's a train station, with people running and sitting.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The bustling morning station scene sets a mood — though no weather is mentioned, the environment shapes the feeling.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: Everyone wants to go somewhere but is waiting — the same routine will repeat tomorrow.

title: The Hospital at Night
text_zh: 晚上十点，医院里还有人。医生在工作，他们很忙。一个医生对我说：「你可以回家了。」我说：「谢谢。」他没有说「再见」，他已经在看下一个人了。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shí diǎn, yīyuàn lǐ hái yǒu rén. Yīshēng zài gōngzuò, tāmen hěn máng. Yí gè yīshēng duì wǒ shuō:「Nǐ kěyǐ huí jiā le.」Wǒ shuō:「Xièxie.」Tā méiyǒu shuō「zàijiàn」, tā yǐjīng zài kàn xià yí gè rén le.
text_en: At ten at night, there are still people in the hospital. Doctors are working — they're busy. One doctor says to me: 'You can go home now.' I say: 'Thank you.' He doesn't say goodbye — he's already looking at the next person.

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: The doctor works late at night, already looking at the next patient — that dedication reflects deep values.

questions.q_en: What did the doctor say to me?
questions.explanation: Look for 你可以回家了 (You can go home now) in the third sentence — 医生对我说： — the doctor.

questions.q_zh: 医生为什么没有说「再见」？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the doctor say goodbye?

options.text: 他已经在看下一个人了
options.pinyin: tā yǐjīng zài kàn xià yí gè rén le
options.text_en: He's already seeing the next person

questions.explanation: The clue is 他已经在看下 (He's already seeing the next person) in the closing line — he's already looking at the next person — no time.

title: The School After Hours
text_zh: 下午五点，学生都走了。学校里很安静。一个老师还在里面，他在看学生写的字。他看了很多，一个一个地看。他看得很认真。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ wǔ diǎn, xuéshēng dōu zǒu le. Xuéxiào lǐ hěn ānjìng. Yí gè lǎoshī hái zài lǐmiàn, tā zài kàn xuéshēng xiě de zì. Tā kàn le hěn duō, yí gè yí gè de kàn. Tā kàn de hěn rènzhēn.
text_en: At five in the afternoon, the students have all left. The school is quiet. One teacher is still inside, reading the characters the students wrote. He reads many of them, one by one. He reads very carefully.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: A teacher staying alone after hours to read student work one by one — quiet dedication in an ordinary setting.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The teacher quietly reviews work after everyone leaves — this steady routine will simply continue.

questions.q_en: How does the teacher read them?

options.pinyin: yí gè yí gè de kàn

questions.explanation: Look for 一个一个地看 (One by one) in the passage — he reads them one by.

title: Buying Water at the Airport
text_zh: 在机场，一瓶水要二十块钱。外面的商店只要三块。我问：「为什么这么贵？」他说：「这是机场。」我买了。我不高兴，但是我很渴。
text_pinyin: Zài jīchǎng, yì píng shuǐ yào èrshí kuài qián. Wàimiàn de shāngdiàn zhǐ yào sān kuài. Wǒ wèn:「Wèishénme zhème guì?」Tā shuō:「Zhè shì jīchǎng.」Wǒ mǎi le. Wǒ bù gāoxìng, dànshì wǒ hěn kě.
text_en: At the airport, a bottle of water costs twenty yuan. Outside shops, it's only three. I ask: 'Why so expensive?' He says: 'This is the airport.' I buy it. I'm not happy, but I'm very thirsty.

questions.q_en: How much is the water at the airport?
questions.explanation: Look for 二十块 (Twenty yuan) in the opening line — a bottle of water costs twenty yuan at the airport.

questions.q_en: Did I buy the water?
questions.explanation: Look for 买了 (Yes) in the passage — I buy it, even though I'm not happy about the price.

questions.q_en: Why did I buy it?

options.text_en: Because my friend wants to drink

questions.explanation: 我问： — I'm very thirsty — thirst outweighed the high price.

title: The New Teacher
text_zh: 我们有一个新老师。她很年轻，但是她说汉语说得非常好。第一天，她对我们说：「请叫我王老师。」她说话不多，但是说的都很有用。同学们都很喜欢她。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen yǒu yí gè xīn lǎoshī. Tā hěn niánqīng, dànshì tā shuō Hànyǔ shuō de fēicháng hǎo. Dì yī tiān, tā duì wǒmen shuō:「Qǐng jiào wǒ Wáng lǎoshī.」Tā shuōhuà bù duō, dànshì shuō de dōu hěn yǒuyòng. Tóngxuémen dōu hěn xǐhuan tā.
text_en: We have a new teacher. She's young, but she speaks Chinese extremely well. On the first day, she says to us: 'Please call me Teacher Wang.' She doesn't talk much, but everything she says is useful. The classmates all like her a lot.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: The new teacher speaks little but says only useful things — this subtly suggests quality over quantity, an inner shift.

questions.q_en: Does the teacher talk a lot?
questions.explanation: Look for 不多 (Not much) in the middle of the passage — she doesn't talk much.

questions.q_en: Do the classmates like her?
questions.explanation: Look for 都很喜欢 (All like her a lot) in the closing line — the classmates all like her a lot.

title: Who Drank My Tea?
text_zh: 早上，我做了一杯茶。我去了一下外面。回来的时候，茶少了。谁喝了？家里只有我和猫。猫不喝茶。那是谁？我看了看猫，猫看了看我。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang, wǒ zuò le yì bēi chá. Wǒ qù le yíxià wàimiàn. Huílái de shíhou, chá shǎo le. Shéi hē le? Jiā lǐ zhǐ yǒu wǒ hé māo. Māo bù hē chá. Nà shì shéi? Wǒ kàn le kàn māo, māo kàn le kàn wǒ.
text_en: In the morning, I make a cup of tea. I step outside briefly. When I come back, there's less tea. Who drank it? At home there's only me and the cat. Cats don't drink tea. Then who was it? I look at the cat. The cat looks at me.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The narrator enjoys tea, a cat, and small mysteries — this gentle life will just keep going.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The narrator puzzles over missing tea and exchanges looks with the cat — showing curiosity and gentle humor.

questions.q_en: Do I know who drank the tea?
questions.explanation: The passage ends without an answer — the narrator still doesn't know who drank the tea.

title: The Phone Call
text_zh: 昨天晚上，有人打电话给我。我说：「你好，你是谁？」那个人没有说话。我又说：「请问你是谁？」他还是没有说话。然后，电话关了。今天，他又打了。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshang, yǒu rén dǎ diànhuà gěi wǒ. Wǒ shuō:「Nǐ hǎo, nǐ shì shéi?」Nàge rén méiyǒu shuōhuà. Wǒ yòu shuō:「Qǐngwèn nǐ shì shéi?」Tā háishi méiyǒu shuōhuà. Ránhòu, diànhuà guān le. Jīntiān, tā yòu dǎ le.
text_en: Last night, someone called me. I said: 'Hello, who are you?' The person didn't speak. I said again: 'May I ask who you are?' They still didn't speak. Then the phone went dead. Today, they called again.

questions.q_en: Did the person speak?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有说话 (Didn't speak) in the third sentence — the person didn't speak both times.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The narrator wonders who called and feels unsettled — showing an emotional inner response to the mystery.

questions.q_en: What happened today?
questions.explanation: Look for 他又打了 (That person called again) in the final sentence — today, they called again.

title: The Missing Book
text_zh: 我的书不在桌子上了。昨天它还在。我问同学：「你看到我的书了吗？」同学说：「没有。」我在家里到处找。最后，我在猫的下面找到了。猫正在上面睡觉。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de shū bú zài zhuōzi shàng le. Zuótiān tā hái zài. Wǒ wèn tóngxué:「Nǐ kàn dào wǒ de shū le ma?」Tóngxué shuō:「Méiyǒu.」Wǒ zài jiā lǐ dàochù zhǎo. Zuìhòu, wǒ zài māo de xiàmiàn zhǎo dào le. Māo zhèngzài shàngmiàn shuìjiào.
text_en: My book isn't on the table anymore. Yesterday it was still there. I ask a classmate: 'Did you see my book?' The classmate says: 'No.' I search everywhere at home. Finally, I find it under the cat. The cat is sleeping on top of it.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: A missing book found under a sleeping cat — the understated mystery creates a soft, reflective tone.

questions.q_en: Did the classmate see the book?
questions.explanation: The classmate says no, they didn't see the book.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: The book is under a sleeping cat, the search is calm — the whole scene feels hushed and reflective.

title: Two Languages
text_zh: 我说英语，也在学汉语。说英语的时候，我是一个人。说汉语的时候，我好像是另一个人。说汉语的我说话少，但是想的多。我不知道哪个是真的我。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shuō Yīngyǔ, yě zài xué Hànyǔ. Shuō Yīngyǔ de shíhou, wǒ shì yí gè rén. Shuō Hànyǔ de shíhou, wǒ hǎoxiàng shì lìng yí gè rén. Shuō Hànyǔ de wǒ shuōhuà shǎo, dànshì xiǎng de duō. Wǒ bù zhīdào nǎge shì zhēn de wǒ.
text_en: I speak English and am also learning Chinese. When I speak English, I'm one person. When I speak Chinese, I seem to be another person. The Chinese-speaking me talks less but thinks more. I don't know which one is the real me.

questions.q_en: What am I learning?
questions.explanation: Look for 我说英语， (Chinese) in the opening line — I speak English and am also learning Chinese.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: Speaking Chinese makes the narrator talk less but think more — a different language brings inner change.

questions.q_zh: 我知道哪个是真的我吗？
questions.q_en: Do I know which one is the real me?
questions.explanation: Look for 不知道 (Don't know) in the closing line — I don't know which one is the real me.

title: Not From Here
text_zh: 我住在北京，但是我不是中国人。有人问我：「你是哪国人？」我说了，他们说：「你的汉语很好！」我说：「谢谢。」但是我想说的是：我住在这里，这里也是我的家。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài Běijīng, dànshì wǒ bú shì Zhōngguó rén. Yǒu rén wèn wǒ:「Nǐ shì nǎ guó rén?」Wǒ shuō le, tāmen shuō:「Nǐ de Hànyǔ hěn hǎo!」Wǒ shuō:「Xièxie.」Dànshì wǒ xiǎng shuō de shì: wǒ zhù zài zhèlǐ, zhèlǐ yě shì wǒ de jiā.
text_en: I live in Beijing, but I'm not Chinese. People ask me: 'Where are you from?' I answer, and they say: 'Your Chinese is good!' I say: 'Thank you.' But what I want to say is: I live here — this is my home too.

questions.q_zh: 对话中没有说出来的是什么？
questions.q_en: What is left unsaid in the conversation?
questions.explanation: The narrator says thank you outwardly but wants to say 'this is my home too' — the real feeling stays unspoken.

questions.q_en: What do people say to me?
questions.explanation: Look for 你的汉语很好 (Your Chinese is very good) in the third sentence — people tell the narrator:.

questions.q_en: What do I want to say?

options.pinyin: zhèlǐ yě shì wǒ de jiā

questions.explanation: Look for 这里也是我的 (This is my home too) in the closing line — what I want to say is:.

title: My Name
text_zh: 我有两个名字。一个是英语名字，一个是中文名字。朋友叫我英语名字。老师叫我中文名字。有时候我听到中文名字，要想一下：那是在叫我吗？
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Yí gè shì Yīngyǔ míngzi, yí gè shì Zhōngwén míngzi. Péngyou jiào wǒ Yīngyǔ míngzi. Lǎoshī jiào wǒ Zhōngwén míngzi. Yǒu shíhou wǒ tīng dào Zhōngwén míngzi, yào xiǎng yíxià: nà shì zài jiào wǒ ma?
text_en: I have two names. One is an English name, one is a Chinese name. Friends call me by my English name. Teachers call me by my Chinese name. Sometimes when I hear the Chinese name, I have to think for a moment: is that calling me?

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: Friends use one name, teachers another — both groups know the narrator well enough to use the right name.

questions.q_en: Who calls me by my Chinese name?
questions.explanation: Look for 老师 (Teachers) in the passage — teachers call me by my Chinese name.

questions.q_zh: 听到中文名字的时候，我怎么样？
questions.q_en: What happens when I hear my Chinese name?

options.text: 要想一下那是不是在叫我
options.pinyin: yào xiǎng yíxià nà shì bu shì zài jiào wǒ
options.text_en: Have to think if it's calling me

questions.explanation: 要想一下： — sometimes hearing the Chinese name, the narrator has to think: is that calling me?

title: The Cat's Schedule
text_zh: 我的猫早上睡觉，中午睡觉，下午也睡觉。晚上，它起来了。它看着我，好像在说：「你为什么不睡觉？」我说：「因为我要工作。」猫不明白。它又睡了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de māo zǎoshang shuìjiào, zhōngwǔ shuìjiào, xiàwǔ yě shuìjiào. Wǎnshang, tā qǐlái le. Tā kànzhe wǒ, hǎoxiàng zài shuō:「Nǐ wèishénme bú shuìjiào?」Wǒ shuō:「Yīnwèi wǒ yào gōngzuò.」Māo bù míngbai. Tā yòu shuì le.
text_en: My cat sleeps in the morning, sleeps at noon, sleeps in the afternoon too. At night, it gets up. It looks at me as if saying: 'Why aren't you sleeping?' I say: 'Because I have to work.' The cat doesn't understand. It goes back to sleep.

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: The cat sleeps all day while the narrator works — their different lifestyles reflect different priorities.

questions.q_zh: 这段话对工作态度的看法是什么？
questions.q_en: What view of work attitude does this passage present?

options.text_en: work is less important than life

questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I have to work' while the cat just sleeps — doing your work matters even if the cat disagrees.

questions.q_en: What did the cat do in the end?
questions.explanation: Look for 又睡了 (Went back to sleep) in the closing line — the cat doesn't understand and goes back to sleep.

title: Learning to Say Thank You
text_zh: 我学汉语的第一天，老师说：「先学一个词：谢谢。」我说：「谢谢。」老师说：「很好。」我说：「谢谢。」老师说：「不用谢。」我说：「谢谢。」老师笑了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ xué Hànyǔ de dì yī tiān, lǎoshī shuō:「Xiān xué yí gè cí: xièxie.」Wǒ shuō:「Xièxie.」Lǎoshī shuō:「Hěn hǎo.」Wǒ shuō:「Xièxie.」Lǎoshī shuō:「Búyòng xiè.」Wǒ shuō:「Xièxie.」Lǎoshī xiào le.
text_en: On my first day learning Chinese, the teacher says: 'First learn one word: thank you.' I say: 'Thank you.' The teacher says: 'Very good.' I say: 'Thank you.' The teacher says: 'You're welcome.' I say: 'Thank you.' The teacher laughs.

questions.q_en: What word was learned on the first day?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 谢谢 — the teacher tells them: first learn one word — thank you.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: The student keeps saying thank you and the teacher laughs — it's a gentle, warm moment between them.

questions.q_en: What did the teacher do in the end?
questions.explanation: Look for 笑了 (Laughed) in the closing line — the teacher laughed at the end.

title: Dinner for One
text_zh: 晚上，我一个人去饭店吃饭。我要了米饭、一个菜、一杯茶。饭店里的人都是两个人、三个人。只有我一个人。但是菜很好吃。一个人吃饭，也可以很好。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang, wǒ yí gè rén qù fàndiàn chī fàn. Wǒ yào le mǐfàn, yí gè cài, yì bēi chá. Fàndiàn lǐ de rén dōu shì liǎng gè rén, sān gè rén. Zhǐ yǒu wǒ yí gè rén. Dànshì cài hěn hǎochī. Yí gè rén chī fàn, yě kěyǐ hěn hǎo.
text_en: In the evening, I go to a restaurant alone to eat. I order rice, one dish, and a cup of tea. Everyone in the restaurant is in groups of two, three. I'm the only one alone. But the food is delicious. Eating alone can be good too.

questions.q_en: What did I order?
questions.explanation: Look for 米饭菜茶 (Rice, a dish, tea) — 我要了米饭、一个菜、 the order was rice, one dish, and a cup of tea.

questions.q_zh: 饭店里的人都是怎么样的？
questions.q_en: How are the people in the restaurant grouped?

options.pinyin: liǎng gè rén, sān gè rén

questions.explanation: Look for 两个人三个人 (In twos and threes) — everyone is in groups of two or three.

questions.q_zh: 我觉得一个人吃饭怎么样？
questions.q_en: How do I feel about eating alone?
questions.explanation: The clue is 也可以很好 (Can be good too) in the closing line — eating alone can be good too.

title: The Night Market Closes
text_zh: 晚上十点，夜市的人少了。一个卖水果的人在收东西。他的苹果没有卖完。他看了看，把三个苹果给了旁边卖茶的人。那个人说「谢谢」，笑了。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shí diǎn, yèshì de rén shǎo le. Yí gè mài shuǐguǒ de rén zài shōu dōngxi. Tā de píngguǒ méiyǒu mài wán. Tā kàn le kàn, bǎ sān gè píngguǒ gěi le pángbiān mài chá de rén. Nà ge rén shuō「xièxie」, xiào le.
text_en: At ten at night, the night market has fewer people. A fruit seller is packing up. His apples didn't all sell. He looked around, then gave three apples to the tea seller next to him. That person said 'thank you' and smiled.

questions.q_en: Did all the fruit sell?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有卖完 (not all sold) in the third sentence — his apples didn't all sell.

questions.q_en: Who did he give the apples to?
questions.explanation: Look for 卖茶的人 (the tea seller) in the passage — he gave three apples to the tea seller next to him.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: A vendor giving unsold fruit to a neighbor who smiles and says thank you — a small, warm gesture.

title: Two Chairs on the Sidewalk
text_zh: 我家楼下有两把椅子。每天下午，两个老人坐在那里。他们不怎么说话，就是坐着，看人走过。有时候喝茶。今天下雨了，椅子在，人不在。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu liǎng bǎ yǐzi. Měi tiān xiàwǔ, liǎng gè lǎorén zuò zài nàlǐ. Tāmen bù zěnme shuō huà, jiùshì zuòzhe, kàn rén zǒu guò. Yǒu shíhou hē chá. Jīntiān xià yǔ le, yǐzi zài, rén bú zài.
text_en: Downstairs from my home there are two chairs. Every afternoon, two old people sit there. They don't talk much, just sit and watch people walk by. Sometimes they drink tea. Today it rained — the chairs are there, but the people aren't.

questions.q_en: Did the old people come today?
questions.explanation: Look for 没来 (no) — today it rained — the chairs are there, but the people aren't.

questions.q_en: What do they usually do?
questions.explanation: Look for 看人走过 (watch people walk by) in the third sentence — they sit and watch people walk by.

questions.q_en: Why aren't the people there today?
questions.explanation: The clue is 下雨了 (it rained) in the closing line — which tells us today it rained, which is why the old people didn't come.

title: The Coffee Shop at the Corner
text_zh: 路口有一个很小的咖啡店。里面只能坐五个人。每天早上，很多人来买咖啡，但是不坐下。他们买了就走。只有一个女人每天都坐在里面，用电脑工作。
text_pinyin: Lùkǒu yǒu yí gè hěn xiǎo de kāfēidiàn. Lǐmiàn zhǐ néng zuò wǔ gè rén. Měi tiān zǎoshang, hěn duō rén lái mǎi kāfēi, dànshì bú zuò xià. Tāmen mǎi le jiù zǒu. Zhǐ yǒu yí gè nǚrén měi tiān dōu zuò zài lǐmiàn, yòng diànnǎo gōngzuò.
text_en: At the intersection there's a small coffee shop. It can only seat five people. Every morning, many people come to buy coffee but don't sit down. They buy and leave. Only one woman sits inside every day, working on her computer.

questions.q_en: Is the coffee shop big?
questions.explanation: Look for 很小 — it directly confirms very small.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: One woman sits there daily working on her computer while others rush in and out — a cozy, steady presence.

questions.q_zh: 很多人买了咖啡以后怎么样？
questions.q_en: What do most people do after buying coffee?
questions.explanation: Look for 买了就走 (buy and leave) in the passage — most people buy and leave without sitting down.

title: Sound of the City at Night
text_zh: 晚上十一点了。外面还有声音。楼下有人在说话。远的地方有出租车的声音。一只狗叫了两下，然后安静了。我关了灯，听着这些声音睡觉。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shíyī diǎn le. Wàimiàn hái yǒu shēngyīn. Lóu xià yǒu rén zài shuō huà. Yuǎn de dìfang yǒu chūzūchē de shēngyīn. Yì zhī gǒu jiào le liǎng xià, ránhòu ānjìng le. Wǒ guān le dēng, tīngzhe zhèxiē shēngyīn shuìjiào.
text_en: It's eleven at night. There are still sounds outside. Downstairs someone is talking. In the distance there's the sound of a taxi. A dog barked twice, then it was quiet. I turned off the light and fell asleep listening to these sounds.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: The narrator falls asleep listening to ordinary nighttime sounds — finding comfort in everyday moments.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' do in the end?

options.text_en: turned off the light and slept

questions.explanation: Look for 我关了灯 — it directly confirms turned off the light and slept.

questions.q_en: What sound is in the distance?
questions.explanation: Look for 出租车 (taxi) in the passage — in the distance there's the sound of a taxi.

title: The Umbrella Stand
text_zh: 商店门口有很多伞。下雨的时候，人们把伞放在那里，然后进去买东西。今天有人走的时候拿错了伞。他拿了一把大的，他的是小的。那把小伞还在那里。
text_pinyin: Shāngdiàn ménkǒu yǒu hěn duō sǎn. Xià yǔ de shíhou, rénmen bǎ sǎn fàng zài nàlǐ, ránhòu jìn qù mǎi dōngxi. Jīntiān yǒu rén zǒu de shíhou ná cuò le sǎn. Tā ná le yì bǎ dà de, tā de shì xiǎo de. Nà bǎ xiǎo sǎn hái zài nàlǐ.
text_en: There are many umbrellas at the shop entrance. When it rains, people put their umbrellas there, then go in to buy things. Today someone took the wrong umbrella when leaving. He took a big one — his was small. That small umbrella is still there.

questions.q_en: What umbrella did the person take?
questions.explanation: Look for 一把大伞 (a big umbrella) — someone took a big one — his was small, but he grabbed.

questions.q_zh: 人们为什么把伞放在门口？
questions.q_en: Why do people put umbrellas at the entrance?
questions.explanation: The key is 然后进去买东, which tells us people put their umbrellas there, then go in to buy things.

questions.q_en: What's the weather like today?
questions.explanation: Look for 下雨 (raining) in the second sentence — it's raining — that's why people have umbrellas.

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 医院里有很多人在等。一个老人坐在那里看书。一个妈妈和她的小孩坐在旁边。小孩不高兴，想回家。妈妈说「再等一下」。老人看了看小孩，笑了。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn lǐ yǒu hěn duō rén zài děng. Yí gè lǎorén zuò zài nàlǐ kàn shū. Yí gè māma hé tā de xiǎohái zuò zài pángbiān. Xiǎohái bù gāoxìng, xiǎng huí jiā. Māma shuō「zài děng yíxià」. Lǎorén kàn le kàn xiǎohái, xiào le.
text_en: Many people are waiting at the hospital. An old man sits there reading. A mother and her child sit nearby. The child is unhappy and wants to go home. The mother says 'wait a little longer.' The old man looks at the child and smiles.

questions.q_zh: 从这里可以看出他/她什么样的性格？
questions.q_en: What does this reveal about their personality?
questions.explanation: The mother tells the child to wait and the old man smiles at the child — both show focused purpose in their actions.

questions.q_zh: 这段话想要读者从中获得什么？
questions.q_en: What does the passage want the reader to gain from it?

options.text: 一种新的看事物的方式
options.pinyin: yì zhǒng xīn de kàn shìwù de fāngshì
options.text_en: a new way of seeing things

questions.explanation: The old man reading, the child wanting to leave, waiting in the hospital — each detail offers a fresh angle on patience.

questions.q_en: What did the mother say?
questions.explanation: Look for 再等一下 (wait a little longer) in the passage — the mother told the child to wait a bit more.

title: The School Gate at Three
text_zh: 下午三点，学校门口有很多人。他们是学生的爸爸妈妈。他们每天都在这里等。三点十分，学校开门了。学生们跑出来。一个小女孩看到妈妈，很高兴，跑过去。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sān diǎn, xuéxiào ménkǒu yǒu hěn duō rén. Tāmen shì xuéshēng de bàba māma. Tāmen měi tiān dōu zài zhèlǐ děng. Sān diǎn shí fēn, xuéxiào kāi mén le. Xuéshēngmen pǎo chūlái. Yí gè xiǎo nǚhái kàn dào māma, hěn gāoxìng, pǎo guòqù.
text_en: At three in the afternoon, many people are at the school gate. They are the students' parents. They wait here every day. At three ten, the school opens its doors. Students run out. A little girl sees her mother, is happy, and runs over.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的节奏感对阅读体验有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the pacing of this passage affect the reading experience?

options.text: 让读者放慢速度去感受
options.pinyin: ràng dúzhě fàng màn sùdù qù gǎnshòu
options.text_en: slows the reader down to feel

options.pinyin: ràng dúzhě xiǎng kuài diǎn dú wán
options.text_en: makes the reader want to finish quickly

questions.explanation: The passage builds slowly — parents waiting, doors opening, a girl running — making you feel each moment unfold.

questions.q_en: What time does the school open?
questions.explanation: Look for 三点十分 (three ten) in the passage — at three ten, the school opens.

questions.q_zh: 小女孩看到妈妈的时候怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the little girl feel when she sees her mother?
questions.explanation: The clue is 小女孩看到妈 — the little girl sees her mother, is happy, and runs over.

title: The Library Rule
text_zh: 图书馆里不能说话。一个学生在打电话。一个老师走过去，没有说什么，就看着他。那个学生看到老师了，说「对不起」，然后出去打电话了。
text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn lǐ bù néng shuō huà. Yí gè xuéshēng zài dǎ diànhuà. Yí gè lǎoshī zǒu guòqù, méiyǒu shuō shénme, jiù kànzhe tā. Nà ge xuéshēng kàn dào lǎoshī le, shuō「duìbuqǐ」, ránhòu chū qù dǎ diànhuà le.
text_en: You can't talk in the library. A student is making a phone call. A teacher walks over and doesn't say anything, just looks at him. The student sees the teacher, says 'sorry,' then goes outside to make the call.

questions.q_en: Can you talk in the library?
questions.explanation: Look for 不能 (no) in the opening line — you can't talk in the library.

questions.q_en: What did the teacher do?

options.pinyin: méi shuō shénme, jiù kànzhe tā
options.text_en: said nothing, just looked at him

questions.explanation: Look for 没有说什么 — it directly confirms said nothing, just looked at him.

questions.q_zh: 观察的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the act of observing suggest?
questions.explanation: The teacher corrected behavior with just a look, no words — that quiet noticing shows attentiveness to detail.

title: The Train Station Clock
text_zh: 火车站有一个很大的钟。很多人看那个钟。有的人看了钟以后跑起来——他们的火车快要走了。有的人看了钟以后坐下来——还有时间。那个钟每天看很多人来，很多人走。
text_pinyin: Huǒchēzhàn yǒu yí gè hěn dà de zhōng. Hěn duō rén kàn nà ge zhōng. Yǒude rén kàn le zhōng yǐhòu pǎo qǐlái——tāmen de huǒchē kuài yào zǒu le. Yǒude rén kàn le zhōng yǐhòu zuò xiàlái——hái yǒu shíjiān. Nà ge zhōng měi tiān kàn hěn duō rén lái, hěn duō rén zǒu.
text_en: The train station has a big clock. Many people look at that clock. Some people start running after looking — their train is about to leave. Some people sit down after looking — there's still time. That clock watches many people come and many people go every day.

questions.q_en: Why do some people start running?

options.text_en: the train is about to leave

questions.explanation: Look for 火车站 — some people start running after looking — their train is about to leave.

questions.q_en: Why do some people sit down?
questions.explanation: The key is 钟以后坐下, which tells us some people sit down after looking — there's still time.

questions.q_zh: 这段话如何处理'孤独'这个概念？
questions.q_en: How does this passage treat the concept of 'solitude'?

options.pinyin: bǎ tā dàng zuò yì zhǒng xiǎngshòu
options.text_en: treats it as something to enjoy

questions.explanation: The clock watches people come and go all day — it's portrayed as a quiet, solitary witness that just observes.

title: The Restaurant's Best Table
text_zh: 这个饭店有一张桌子在窗旁边。坐在那里可以看到外面的街。很多人都想坐那里。今天我来的时候，那张桌子没有人。我很高兴，坐下来，点了茶和菜。
text_pinyin: Zhè ge fàndiàn yǒu yì zhāng zhuōzi zài chuāng pángbiān. Zuò zài nàlǐ kěyǐ kàn dào wàimiàn de jiē. Hěn duō rén dōu xiǎng zuò nàlǐ. Jīntiān wǒ lái de shíhou, nà zhāng zhuōzi méiyǒu rén. Wǒ hěn gāoxìng, zuò xiàlái, diǎn le chá hé cài.
text_en: This restaurant has a table by the window. Sitting there, you can see the street outside. Many people want to sit there. Today when I came, that table was empty. I was happy, sat down, and ordered tea and food.

questions.q_zh: 读完后，读者最可能有什么感受？
questions.q_en: How is the reader most likely to feel after reading this?
questions.explanation: The narrator sits by the window watching the street with tea — the scene invites quiet thought.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' order?
questions.explanation: Look for 茶和菜 (tea and food) in the closing line — I ordered tea and food.

questions.q_en: Why is 'I' happy?
questions.explanation: Look for 饭店有一张桌 — that table was empty, and the narrator was happy to get.

title: The Post Office Line
text_zh: 邮局里有五个人在排队。前面的人很慢，后面的人不高兴。一个先生看了看手机，叹了口气。一个小姐在看书。每个人都在等，但是每个人等的方法不一样。
text_pinyin: Yóujú lǐ yǒu wǔ gè rén zài pái duì. Qiánmiàn de rén hěn màn, hòumiàn de rén bù gāoxìng. Yí gè xiānsheng kàn le kàn shǒujī, tàn le kǒu qì. Yí gè xiǎojiě zài kàn shū. Měi gè rén dōu zài děng, dànshì měi gè rén děng de fāngfǎ bù yíyàng.
text_en: Five people are in line at the post office. The person at the front is slow, and the people behind are unhappy. A man looks at his phone and sighs. A young woman is reading. Everyone is waiting, but everyone waits differently.

questions.q_zh: 为什么这段话值得仔细读第二遍？
questions.q_en: Why is this passage worth reading a second time?

options.text: 能发现更多隐含的意义
options.pinyin: néng fāxiàn gèng duō yǐnhán de yìyì
options.text_en: you can discover more hidden meanings

options.text: 内容太复杂，一遍读不懂
options.pinyin: nèiróng tài fùzá, yí biàn dú bù dǒng
options.text_en: it's too complex to understand in one reading

options.pinyin: bù zhíde dú dì èr biàn

questions.explanation: Each person waits differently — sighing, reading, being slow — a second read reveals what each behavior suggests.

questions.q_en: What did the man do?

options.pinyin: kàn shǒujī, tàn le kǒu qì

questions.explanation: Look for 叹了口气 (looked at phone and sighed) in the third sentence — a man looks at his phone and sighs.

questions.q_zh: 文中的描写方式有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the descriptive style have?

options.text: 让场景变得立体和生动
options.pinyin: ràng chǎngjǐng biàn de lìtǐ hé shēngdòng
options.text_en: makes the scene vivid and three-dimensional

questions.explanation: Each person's different waiting behavior is described — phone-sighing, reading — making the scene feel real and layered.

title: Who Ate the Apple?
text_zh: 桌子上有三个苹果。我出去了。回来的时候，只有两个了。家里有三个人：我、我的同学、还有他的猫。我的同学说「我没吃」。猫在桌子下面睡觉。但是我看到猫旁边有苹果。
text_pinyin: Zhuōzi shàng yǒu sān gè píngguǒ. Wǒ chū qù le. Huílái de shíhou, zhǐ yǒu liǎng gè le. Jiā lǐ yǒu sān gè rén: wǒ, wǒ de tóngxué, hái yǒu tā de māo. Wǒ de tóngxué shuō「wǒ méi chī」. Māo zài zhuōzi xiàmiàn shuìjiào. Dànshì wǒ kàn dào māo pángbiān yǒu píngguǒ.
text_en: There were three apples on the table. I went out. When I came back, there were only two. Three are in the house: me, my classmate, and his cat. My classmate says 'I didn't eat it.' The cat is sleeping under the table. But I see an apple next to the cat.

questions.q_en: Who probably took the apple?
questions.explanation: There's an apple right next to the cat — the cat is the most likely culprit.

questions.q_zh: 做饭/吃饭的场景暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the eating/cooking scene imply?
questions.explanation: Three people sharing a home with food around them — even a mystery about an apple carries daily-life warmth.

questions.q_zh: 做饭/吃饭的场景暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the eating/cooking scene imply?
questions.explanation: Apples, a classmate, a sleeping cat — the scene is domestic and lighthearted despite the mini-mystery.

title: The Missing Book
text_zh: 我的书不见了。昨天晚上还在桌子上。今天早上起床，书不在了。我问了同学：「你看到我的书了吗？」他说没有。但是他的桌子上有一本书，和我的很像。我看了看——那就是我的书。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de shū bújiàn le. Zuótiān wǎnshang hái zài zhuōzi shàng. Jīntiān zǎoshang qǐchuáng, shū bú zài le. Wǒ wèn le tóngxué:「nǐ kàn dào wǒ de shū le ma?」Tā shuō méiyǒu. Dànshì tā de zhuōzi shàng yǒu yì běn shū, hé wǒ de hěn xiàng. Wǒ kàn le kàn——nà jiùshì wǒ de shū.
text_en: My book is missing. Last night it was still on the table. This morning when I got up, the book was gone. I asked my classmate: 'Did you see my book?' He said no. But on his table there's a book that looks like mine. I looked — that is my book.

questions.q_zh: 为什么这段话值得仔细读第二遍？
questions.q_en: Why is this passage worth reading a second time?

options.text: 能发现更多隐含的意义
options.pinyin: néng fāxiàn gèng duō yǐnhán de yìyì
options.text_en: you can discover more hidden meanings

options.text: 内容太复杂，一遍读不懂
options.pinyin: nèiróng tài fùzá, yí biàn dú bù dǒng
options.text_en: it's too complex to understand in one reading

options.pinyin: bù zhíde dú dì èr biàn

questions.explanation: The classmate said no, but the book is on his table — a second reading reveals the contradiction more clearly.

questions.q_en: What did the classmate say?
questions.explanation: The classmate said no when asked about the book.

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: Finding the missing book on the classmate's table stirs a mix of confusion and recognition — an echo of past trust.

title: The Open Window
text_zh: 我出去工作以前，关了所有的窗。回来的时候，一个窗开着。桌子上有水。外面没有下雨。我的猫坐在窗旁边，看着我。我想我知道了——猫开了窗。
text_pinyin: Wǒ chū qù gōngzuò yǐqián, guān le suǒyǒu de chuāng. Huílái de shíhou, yí gè chuāng kāizhe. Zhuōzi shàng yǒu shuǐ. Wàimiàn méiyǒu xià yǔ. Wǒ de māo zuò zài chuāng pángbiān, kànzhe wǒ. Wǒ xiǎng wǒ zhīdào le——māo kāi le chuāng.
text_en: Before I left for work, I closed all the windows. When I came back, one window was open. There's water on the table. It's not raining outside. My cat sits by the window, looking at me. I think I know — the cat opened the window.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' do before leaving?
questions.explanation: Look for 关了窗 (closed windows) — before leaving for work, I closed all the windows.

questions.q_en: What's on the table?
questions.explanation: Look for 水 (water) in the third sentence — there's water on the table.

questions.q_en: Who does 'I' think opened the window?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 猫 — I think I know — the cat opened the window.

title: Two Cups of Tea
text_zh: 我回到家，桌子上有两杯茶。一杯热的，一杯冷的。但是家里没有人。冷的那杯已经喝了一些。热的那杯是新的。有人来过，然后走了。但是谁呢？我看到桌子上有一个字条。
text_pinyin: Wǒ huí dào jiā, zhuōzi shàng yǒu liǎng bēi chá. Yì bēi rè de, yì bēi lěng de. Dànshì jiā lǐ méiyǒu rén. Lěng de nà bēi yǐjīng hē le yìxiē. Rè de nà bēi shì xīn de. Yǒu rén lái guò, ránhòu zǒu le. Dànshì shéi ne? Wǒ kàn dào zhuōzi shàng yǒu yí gè zìtiáo.
text_en: I come home and there are two cups of tea on the table. One hot, one cold. But nobody is home. The cold one has already been partly drunk. The hot one is new. Someone came and left. But who? I see a note on the table.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: Two cups of tea — one cold, one fresh — show someone came and left, giving the narrator new understanding of what happened.

questions.q_en: Is anyone home?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 没有人 — nobody is home.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' see?
questions.explanation: Look for 一个字条 (a note) in the closing line — I see a note on the table.

title: The Dog Knows Something
text_zh: 每天晚上，我的狗都很安静。但是今天晚上它不安静。它一直看着门。我打开门，外面没有人。但是门前面有一个小东西。我拿起来看——是一个很旧的钥匙。这是谁的？
text_pinyin: Měi tiān wǎnshang, wǒ de gǒu dōu hěn ānjìng. Dànshì jīntiān wǎnshang tā bù ānjìng. Tā yìzhí kànzhe mén. Wǒ dǎkāi mén, wàimiàn méiyǒu rén. Dànshì mén qiánmiàn yǒu yí gè xiǎo dōngxi. Wǒ ná qǐlái kàn——shì yí gè hěn jiù de yàoshi. Zhè shì shéi de?
text_en: Every evening, my dog is quiet. But tonight it's not quiet. It keeps looking at the door. I open the door — nobody is outside. But in front of the door there's a small thing. I pick it up — it's a old key. Whose is this?

questions.q_en: What's in front of the door?
questions.explanation: Look for 我拿起来看—是一个 (a key) in the middle of the passage — in front of the door there's a small thing — it's an old key.

questions.q_en: How is the dog usually at night?
questions.explanation: Look for 很安静 (very quiet) in the opening line — it directly confirms very quiet.

questions.q_zh: 如果另一个人来到同样的场景，会有同样的感受吗？
questions.q_en: Would another person in the same setting have the same feelings?

options.text: 不一定，取决于个人经历
options.pinyin: bù yídìng, qǔjué yú gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: not necessarily, it depends on personal experience

questions.explanation: Finding a mysterious key could spark curiosity, fear, or indifference — it depends on who finds.

title: My Name in Two Languages
text_zh: 我有两个名字。英语名字是我爸爸妈妈给的。中文名字是我的中文老师给的。在学校，老师叫我中文名字。回到家，妈妈叫我英语名字。两个名字都是我，但是感觉不一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Yīngyǔ míngzi shì wǒ bàba māma gěi de. Zhōngwén míngzi shì wǒ de Zhōngwén lǎoshī gěi de. Zài xuéxiào, lǎoshī jiào wǒ Zhōngwén míngzi. Huí dào jiā, māma jiào wǒ Yīngyǔ míngzi. Liǎng gè míngzi dōu shì wǒ, dànshì gǎnjué bù yíyàng.
text_en: I have two names. My English name was given by my parents. My Chinese name was given by my Chinese teacher. At school, the teacher calls me by my Chinese name. At home, mom calls me by my English name. Both names are me, but they feel different.

questions.q_en: Who gave the Chinese name?
questions.explanation: Look for 中文老师 (Chinese teacher) in the third sentence — the Chinese name was given by the Chinese teacher.

questions.q_zh: 在家里妈妈叫什么名字？
questions.q_en: What name does mom use at home?
questions.explanation: Look for 英语名字 (English name) in the second sentence — at home, mom calls me by my English name.

questions.q_en: Do the two names feel the same?

options.text_en: he doesn't like the Chinese name

questions.explanation: The clue is 不一样 (different) in the closing line — both names are me, but they feel different.

title: Speaking Two Languages
text_zh: 说英语的时候，我说话很快。说汉语的时候，我说话很慢。我的朋友说，说汉语的时候我的声音也不一样。他说「你说汉语的时候，好像是另一个人」。我想了想，可能是的。
text_pinyin: Shuō Yīngyǔ de shíhou, wǒ shuō huà hěn kuài. Shuō Hànyǔ de shíhou, wǒ shuō huà hěn màn. Wǒ de péngyou shuō, shuō Hànyǔ de shíhou wǒ de shēngyīn yě bù yíyàng. Tā shuō「nǐ shuō Hànyǔ de shíhou, hǎoxiàng shì lìng yí gè rén」. Wǒ xiǎng le xiǎng, kěnéng shì de.
text_en: When I speak English, I talk fast. When I speak Chinese, I talk slowly. My friend says my voice is different when I speak Chinese too. He says 'when you speak Chinese, it's like you're a different person.' I thought about it — maybe so.

questions.q_zh: 说汉语的时候，「我」怎么说话？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' speak when speaking Chinese?
questions.explanation: The time marker 很慢 appears in the second sentence — when speaking Chinese, I talk slowly.

questions.q_en: What did the friend say?

options.pinyin: nǐ shuō huà tài màn le
hǎoxiàng shì lìng yí gè rén

questions.explanation: The friend says when you speak Chinese, it's like you're a different person.

questions.q_en: Does 'I' think the friend is right?
questions.explanation: Look for 可能是的 (maybe) in the closing line — I thought about it — maybe so. not certain, just possibly agreeing.

title: What I Like Has Changed
text_zh: 小的时候，我不喜欢喝茶。现在我每天都喝茶。小的时候，我喜欢跑步。现在我喜欢走路。小的时候，我不喜欢一个人在家。现在我很喜欢。人会变的。
text_pinyin: Xiǎo de shíhou, wǒ bù xǐhuan hē chá. Xiànzài wǒ měi tiān dōu hē chá. Xiǎo de shíhou, wǒ xǐhuan pǎobù. Xiànzài wǒ xǐhuan zǒulù. Xiǎo de shíhou, wǒ bù xǐhuan yí gè rén zài jiā. Xiànzài wǒ hěn xǐhuan. Rén huì biàn de.
text_en: When I was young, I didn't like drinking tea. Now I drink tea every day. When I was young, I liked running. Now I like walking. When I was young, I didn't like being home alone. Now I like it. People change.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中什么是'看不见'但能感觉到的？
questions.q_en: What in this passage is 'invisible' but can be felt?

options.text: 人物的情感和内心世界
options.pinyin: rénwù de qínggǎn hé nèixīn shìjiè
options.text_en: the character's emotions and inner world

questions.explanation: Changing preferences over time — tea, walking, being alone — reveal an inner emotional world beneath the surface.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' like when young?
questions.explanation: Look for 跑步 (running) in the third sentence — when I was young, I liked running.

questions.q_zh: 文中哪个细节最能体现作者的态度？
questions.q_en: Which detail best reflects the author's attitude?
questions.explanation: The narrator notices how small preferences changed over time — that's paying attention to everyday shifts.

title: The Accent
text_zh: 我的中文老师是北京人。她说的汉语和别的老师不一样。我学了她的说法。现在我的中国朋友说「你说话有一点北京的感觉」。我没有去过北京，但是北京来到了我的声音里。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de Zhōngwén lǎoshī shì Běijīng rén. Tā shuō de Hànyǔ hé bié de lǎoshī bù yíyàng. Wǒ xué le tā de shuōfǎ. Xiànzài wǒ de Zhōngguó péngyou shuō「nǐ shuō huà yǒu yìdiǎn Běijīng de gǎnjué」. Wǒ méiyǒu qù guò Běijīng, dànshì Běijīng lái dào le wǒ de shēngyīn lǐ.
text_en: My Chinese teacher is from Beijing. Her Chinese is different from other teachers. I learned her way of speaking. Now my Chinese friend says 'you speak with a bit of a Beijing feel.' I've never been to Beijing, but Beijing came into my voice.

questions.q_zh: 这段话和说明文的写法有什么不同？
questions.q_en: How does this passage differ from an expository text?

options.text_en: focuses more on emotion and experience

questions.explanation: The passage is about absorbing a teacher's accent and feeling it become part of you — personal and emotional, not factual.

questions.q_en: Has 'I' been to Beijing?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有去过 (no) in the closing line — I've never been to beijing.

questions.q_zh: 这段话让你想到了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this passage make you think of?
questions.explanation: Absorbing someone's accent without visiting their city — that mirrors how we pick up traits from people around us.

title: The Confident Passenger
text_zh: 我上了车。窗外的路我不认识，但是我没有问。又过了十分钟，外面的楼越来越少，树越来越多。天也暗了。我心里说：没问题，快到了。又过了二十分钟，车停了。司机说「到了，终点站」。我下了车。四面都是山。没有灯，没有人。我站在那里，不知道我在哪里。风很大，很冷。和我心里的感觉一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shàng le chē. Chuāngwài de lù wǒ bú rènshi, dànshì wǒ méiyǒu wèn. Yòu guò le shí fēnzhōng, wàimian de lóu yuè lái yuè shǎo, shù yuè lái yuè duō. Tiān yě àn le. Wǒ xīnli shuō: méi wèntí, kuài dào le. Yòu guò le èrshí fēnzhōng, chē tíng le. Sījī shuō 'dào le, zhōngdiǎnzhàn'. Wǒ xià le chē. Sìmiàn dōu shì shān. Méiyǒu dēng, méiyǒu rén. Wǒ zhàn zài nàlǐ, bù zhīdào wǒ zài nǎlǐ. Fēng hěn dà, hěn lěng. Hé wǒ xīnli de gǎnjué yíyàng.
text_en: I got on the bus. I didn't recognize the road outside, but I didn't ask. Ten more minutes — fewer buildings, more trees. The sky went dark. I told myself: no problem, almost there. Twenty more minutes, the bus stopped. The driver said 'Last stop.' I got off. Mountains on all sides. No lights, no people. I stood there, not knowing where I was. The wind was strong and cold. Same as the feeling inside me.

questions.q_zh: 环境描写和人物心理之间有什么关系？
questions.q_en: What is the relationship between the setting and the character's psychology?

options.text_en: the environment mirrors the inner state

questions.explanation: Mountains, no lights, cold wind — the dark, empty setting matches the narrator's lost, cold feeling inside.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么不直接说出结论？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the author state the conclusion directly?

options.text_en: to let the reader experience it themselves
the author doesn't know the answer

questions.explanation: The passage doesn't state a lesson directly — it lets you feel the narrator's confusion and cold to draw your own conclusion.

questions.q_en: How is 'I' after getting off?
questions.explanation: Look for 不知道在哪里 (doesn't know where they are) — the narrator stands there not knowing where they are.

title: The Cat's Opinion
text_zh: 我给猫买了新的东西吃。很贵的。我放在它前面。它看了看，闻了闻，然后走了。它走到旁边，开始喝水。我说「这个很好吃的！」它看了我一下，又走了。二十块钱的东西，猫不要。
text_pinyin: Wǒ gěi māo mǎi le xīn de dōngxi chī. Hěn guì de. Wǒ fàng zài tā qiánmiàn. Tā kàn le kàn, wén le wén, ránhòu zǒu le. Tā zǒu dào pángbiān, kāishǐ hē shuǐ. Wǒ shuō「zhè ge hěn hǎochī de!」Tā kàn le wǒ yíxià, yòu zǒu le. Èrshí kuài qián de dōngxi, māo búyào.
text_en: I bought the cat new food. Expensive. I put it in front of him. He looked, sniffed, then walked away. He went to the side and started drinking water. I said 'This is really good!' He glanced at me, then walked away again. Twenty yuan of food, and the cat doesn't want it.

questions.q_en: Is the cat's new food expensive?
questions.explanation: Look for 很贵 (very expensive) in the second sentence — the food is expensive — twenty yuan.

questions.q_en: What did the cat do?

options.pinyin: kàn le kàn jiù zǒu le

questions.explanation: Look for 看了看就走了 (looked and walked away) — the cat looked, sniffed, then walked away.

questions.q_en: How much did the food cost?
questions.explanation: Look for 二十块 (twenty yuan) in the closing line — twenty yuan of food.

title: The Phone Call
text_zh: 我的电话响了。一个人说「妈，今天晚上我不回家吃饭了」。我说「我不是你妈妈」。他说「对不起，打错了」。过了十分钟，电话又响了。「妈，今天晚上——」
text_pinyin: Wǒ de diànhuà xiǎng le. Yí gè rén shuō「mā, jīntiān wǎnshang wǒ bù huí jiā chīfàn le」. Wǒ shuō「wǒ búshì nǐ māma」. Tā shuō「duìbuqǐ, dǎ cuò le」. Guò le shí fēnzhōng, tā yòu dǎ lái le. Yòu shuō「mā, jīntiān……」Wǒ shuō「nǐ yòu dǎ cuò le!」Tā shuō「duìbuqǐ duìbuqǐ!」
text_en: My phone rang. Someone said 'Mom, I'm not coming home for dinner tonight.' I said 'I'm not your mother.' He said 'Sorry, wrong number.' Ten minutes later, the phone rang again. 'Mom, tonight—'

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: The caller keeps trying to reach 'Mom' even after a wrong number — the bond between parent and child matters most.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: The caller says 'Mom' and immediately shares dinner plans — they're comfortable enough to skip formalities.

questions.q_zh: 从这里可以看出他/她什么样的性格？
questions.q_en: What does this reveal about their personality?
questions.explanation: The caller dials the wrong number twice but keeps trying — single-mindedly focused on reaching Mom.

title: The Airport at Five AM
text_zh: 早上五点，机场已经有人了。有的人在睡觉，坐在椅子上。有的人在喝咖啡，看手机。一个人拿着很多东西，走得很慢。外面还是黑的。但是机场里面很亮。每个人都要去一个不一样的地方。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang wǔ diǎn, jīchǎng yǐjīng yǒu rén le. Yǒude rén zài shuìjiào, zuò zài yǐzi shàng. Yǒude rén zài hē kāfēi, kàn shǒujī. Yí gè rén názhe hěn duō dōngxi, zǒu de hěn màn. Wàimiàn háishi hēi de. Dànshì jīchǎng lǐmiàn hěn liàng. Měi gè rén dōu yào qù yí gè bù yíyàng de dìfang.
text_en: At five in the morning, there are already people at the airport. Some are sleeping in chairs. Some are drinking coffee, looking at phones. One person carries many things and walks slowly. Outside it's still dark. But inside the airport it's bright. Everyone is going to a different place.

questions.q_en: What's it like outside?
questions.explanation: Look for 还是黑的 (still dark) in the passage — outside it's still dark.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: Seeing people at 5am — sleeping, coffee, walking slowly — gives a new view of how different each traveler's journey.

questions.q_zh: 这些人都去同一个地方吗？
questions.q_en: Are all these people going to the same place?

options.text: 不是，每个人去不一样的地方
options.pinyin: búshì, měi gè rén qù bù yíyàng de dìfang

questions.explanation: Look for 不一样的地方 (no, everyone goes somewhere different) in the closing line — everyone is going to a different place.

title: The Man Who Counts Cars
text_zh: 每天下午，一个老人坐在路边。他看着车，一个一个地数。我问他「你在做什么？」他说「我在看今天有多少红的车。」今天有十二个。
