text_pinyin: Měi tiān xiàwǔ, yí gè lǎorén zuò zài lù biān. Tā kànzhe chē, yí gè yí gè de shǔ. Wǒ wèn tā「nǐ zài zuò shénme?」Tā shuō「wǒ zài kàn jīntiān yǒu duōshǎo hóng de chē.」Jīntiān yǒu shí èr gè.
text_en: Every afternoon, an old man sits by the road. He watches the cars, counting them one by one. I asked him 'What are you doing?' He said 'I'm seeing how many red cars there are today.' Today there were twelve.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: Counting red cars seems trivial, but the narrator finds it worth asking about — small details can carry deeper meaning.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: An old man counting red cars every day — the narrator notices this detail because it hints at something beneath the surface.

questions.q_zh: 观察的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the act of observing suggest?
questions.explanation: Sitting by the road counting red cars every day — that's someone who pays close attention to the small things in life.

title: What She Reads on the Train
text_zh: 火车上，对面的女人在看一本很大的书。我看不到书的名字。她看得很慢，有时候笑一下。到站的时候，她把书放进包里。我看到了——是一本中文书。
text_pinyin: Huǒchē shang, duìmiàn de nǚrén zài kàn yì běn hěn dà de shū. Wǒ kàn bú dào shū de míngzi. Tā kàn de hěn màn, yǒu shíhou xiào yíxià. Dào zhàn de shíhou, tā bǎ shū fàng jìn bāo lǐ. Wǒ kàn dào le——shì yì běn Zhōngwén shū.
text_en: On the train, the woman across from me is reading a big book. I can't see the book's name. She reads slowly, sometimes smiling. When we reached the station, she put the book in her bag. I saw it — it was a Chinese book.

questions.q_en: Why is the narrator curious about the book?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi kàn bú dào shū de míngzi
options.text_en: because they can't see the title

options.pinyin: yīnwèi nǚrén kàn de hěn kuài

options.text_en: because they want to read it too

questions.explanation: Look for 我看不到书 — the narrator can't see the book's name, which makes them curious about what she's reading.

questions.q_zh: 「我」一开始看到书的名字了吗？
questions.q_en: Did 'I' see the book's name at first?

options.pinyin: tā shuō le shū de míngzi

questions.explanation: Look for 看不到 (no, couldn't see) in the second sentence — I can't see the book's name at first.

questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: She reads slowly and smiles at a Chinese book on the train — reading choices reveal what someone values.

title: The Dog That Waits
text_zh: 学校门口有一只狗。每天下午三点，它就来了。它坐在那里，看着出来的人。四点的时候，一个小女孩出来了，狗就跑过去。它每天都这样。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào ménkǒu yǒu yì zhī gǒu. Měi tiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn, tā jiù lái le. Tā zuò zài nàlǐ, kànzhe chūlái de rén. Sì diǎn de shíhou, yí gè xiǎo nǚhái chūlái le, gǒu jiù pǎo guòqù. Tā měi tiān dōu zhèyàng.
text_en: At the school gate there's a dog. Every afternoon at three, it comes. It sits there, watching the people who come out. At four, a little girl comes out, and the dog runs over. It does this every day.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: The dog comes every day at three and waits for the girl — doing this daily shows it matters deeply to the dog.

questions.q_en: What time does the dog come?
questions.explanation: Look for 每天下午三点 (three o'clock) in the second sentence — every afternoon at three, the dog comes.

questions.q_en: Does the dog come every day?
questions.explanation: Look for 每天下午三点 (comes every day) in the second sentence — it does this every day.

title: The Woman at the Window
text_zh: 对面楼的三层，有一个女人每天早上站在窗户边。她手里有一杯茶。她看着下面的人走路，不说话。八点的时候，她就不在了。她可能去上班了。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn lóu de sān céng, yǒu yí gè nǚrén měi tiān zǎoshang zhàn zài chuānghu biān. Tā shǒu lǐ yǒu yì bēi chá. Tā kànzhe xiàmiàn de rén zǒulù, bù shuō huà. Bā diǎn de shíhou, tā jiù bú zài le. Tā kěnéng qù shàngbān le.
text_en: On the third floor of the building across the way, a woman stands by the window every morning. She has a cup of tea in her hand. She watches the people walking below, saying nothing. At eight, she's gone. She probably went to work.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: A woman standing silently with tea every morning, then disappearing by eight — the routine hints at something wistful.

questions.q_en: What floor does she live on?
questions.explanation: Look for 对面楼的三层 — it directly confirms third floor.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: Her solitary morning routine of watching people below in silence suggests a quiet longing or reflective sadness.

title: Two Umbrellas
text_zh: 今天下雨。我在商店门口看到两个人。一个人有伞，一个人没有。有伞的人看了看那个人，把伞给了他。然后自己跑进了雨里。我觉得很有意思。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xià yǔ. Wǒ zài shāngdiàn ménkǒu kàn dào liǎng gè rén. Yí gè rén yǒu sǎn, yí gè rén méiyǒu. Yǒu sǎn de rén kàn le kàn nà ge rén, bǎ sǎn gěi le tā. Ránhòu zìjǐ pǎo jìn le yǔ lǐ. Wǒ juéde hěn yǒu yìsi.
text_en: It's raining today. I saw two people at the shop entrance. One had an umbrella, one didn't. The one with the umbrella looked at the other person and gave them the umbrella. Then ran into the rain themselves. I found it interesting.

questions.q_en: Who ended up with the umbrella?

options.pinyin: yì kāishǐ méiyǒu sǎn de rén
options.text_en: the one who didn't have one at first

options.pinyin: yì kāishǐ yǒu sǎn de rén
options.text_en: the one who had it at first

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 一个人有伞 — the person with the umbrella gave it to the one who didn't have one.

questions.q_en: How is the weather today?
questions.explanation: Look for 下雨 (raining) in the opening line — it's raining today.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' think about this?
questions.explanation: The clue is 我觉得很有意 — I found it interesting.

title: The Same Table
text_zh: 饭馆里有一张桌子在窗户旁边。中午的时候，一个男人在那里吃饭。下午两点，一个女人坐在那里喝茶。晚上，两个朋友在那里说话。一张桌子，三个不一样的时候。
text_pinyin: Fànguǎn lǐ yǒu yì zhāng zhuōzi zài chuānghu pángbiān. Zhōngwǔ de shíhou, yí gè nánrén zài nàlǐ chī fàn. Xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn, yí gè nǚrén zuò zài nàlǐ hē chá. Wǎnshang, liǎng gè péngyou zài nàlǐ shuō huà. Yì zhāng zhuōzi, sān gè bù yíyàng de shíhou.
text_en: In the restaurant there's a table by the window. At noon, a man eats there. At two in the afternoon, a woman sits there drinking tea. In the evening, two friends talk there. One table, three different moments.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断人物是什么样的人？
questions.q_en: What kind of person can we infer the character is?
questions.explanation: Noticing three different people at one table throughout the day takes a careful, patient observer.

questions.q_en: Who was there at noon?
questions.explanation: Look for 中午的时候， (a man) in the second sentence — at noon, a man eats there.

questions.q_zh: 晚上那两个人在做什么？
questions.q_en: What were the two people doing in the evening?
questions.explanation: Look for 说话 (talking) in the passage — in the evening, two friends talk there.

title: The Bus That Comes Late
text_zh: 公共汽车应该八点来。现在八点二十了，还没来。五个人在等。一个人看手机，两个人在说话，一个人在喝咖啡。只有我在看路。车来了，大家都很高兴。
text_pinyin: Gōnggòng qìchē yīnggāi bā diǎn lái. Xiànzài bā diǎn èrshí le, hái méi lái. Wǔ gè rén zài děng. Yí gè rén kàn shǒujī, liǎng gè rén zài shuō huà, yí gè rén zài hē kāfēi. Zhǐ yǒu wǒ zài kàn lù. Chē lái le, dàjiā dōu hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: The bus should come at eight. It's now 8:20 and it still hasn't come. Five people are waiting. One looks at their phone, two are talking, one is drinking coffee. Only I'm watching the road. The bus came, and everyone was happy.

questions.q_zh: 公共汽车晚了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How late was the bus?
questions.explanation: The bus was due at eight and arrived at 8:20 — that's twenty minutes late.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断人物是什么样的人？
questions.q_en: What kind of person can we infer the character is?
questions.explanation: While others look at phones, talk, or drink coffee, the narrator watches the road — showing patience and attentiveness.

questions.q_en: What was 'I' doing?
questions.explanation: Look for 只有我在看路 (watching the road) in the middle of the passage — only I'm watching the road.

title: The Building with No Name
text_zh: 我家旁边有一个很老的楼。门上没有名字。白天没有人进去。但是晚上，里面有灯。我问妈妈那是什么地方。她说「以前是一个商店。」现在不是了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yí gè hěn lǎo de lóu. Mén shang méiyǒu míngzi. Báitiān méiyǒu rén jìnqù. Dànshì wǎnshang, lǐmiàn yǒu dēng. Wǒ wèn māma nà shì shénme dìfang. Tā shuō「yǐqián shì yí gè shāngdiàn.」Xiànzài bú shì le.
text_en: Next to my home there's a old building. There's no name on the door. During the day nobody goes in. But at night, there are lights inside. I asked mom what that place is. She said 'It used to be a shop.' Not anymore.

questions.q_en: What was this building before?
questions.explanation: Mom said it used to be a shop.

questions.q_en: Does anyone go in during the day?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有人 (nobody) in the third sentence — during the day nobody goes.

questions.q_zh: 这个地方对人物有什么意义？
questions.q_en: What significance does this place hold for the character?

options.pinyin: yǒu tèbié de huíyì huò gǎnqíng

questions.explanation: It was once a shop — the building carries the weight of what it used to be.

title: Rain and the Taxi Driver
text_zh: 下雨的时候，出租车很难找。我在路边等了十五分钟。一个出租车来了，里面已经有人了。又等了五分钟，又来了一个。我上了车，说「谢谢，终于有车了。」
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ de shíhou, chūzūchē hěn nán zhǎo. Wǒ zài lù biān děng le shíwǔ fēnzhōng. Yí gè chūzūchē lái le, lǐmiàn yǐjīng yǒu rén le. Yòu děng le wǔ fēnzhōng, yòu lái le yí gè. Wǒ shàng le chē, shuō「xièxie, zhōngyú yǒu chē le.」
text_en: When it rains, taxis are hard to find. I waited by the road for fifteen minutes. A taxi came, but someone was already inside. I waited another five minutes, and another came. I got in and said 'Thank you, finally a car.'

questions.q_en: How long did 'I' wait in total?
questions.explanation: Waited fifteen minutes, then five more — that's twenty minutes total.

questions.q_en: Why are taxis hard to find?
questions.explanation: The clue is 下雨了 (it's raining) — when it rains, taxis are hard to find.

questions.q_zh: 第一个出租车为什么不行？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the first taxi work?
questions.explanation: The clue is 里面已经有人 (someone was already inside) in the third sentence — a taxi came, but someone was already inside.

title: The Quiet Street at Noon
text_zh: 中午十二点半，这条路上没什么人。商店都关门了，大家都去吃饭了。只有一个猫在路上走。太阳很大，猫走到一棵树下面，坐下来了。两点的时候，人又回来了。
text_pinyin: Zhōngwǔ shí èr diǎn bàn, zhè tiáo lù shang méi shénme rén. Shāngdiàn dōu guānmén le, dàjiā dōu qù chī fàn le. Zhǐ yǒu yì gè māo zài lù shang zǒu. Tàiyáng hěn dà, māo zǒu dào yì kē shù xiàmiàn, zuò xiàlái le. Liǎng diǎn de shíhou, rén yòu huílái le.
text_en: At 12:30, there's hardly anyone on this street. The shops are all closed — everyone went to eat. Only a cat is walking on the road. The sun is strong, and the cat walks under a tree and sits down. At two, the people come back.

questions.q_en: Why is there no one on the road?
questions.explanation: The key is 大家都去吃饭, which tells us the shops are all closed — everyone went to eat.

questions.q_en: What did the cat do?

options.pinyin: zǒu dào shù xiàmiàn zuò xiàlái
options.text_en: walked under a tree and sat down

questions.explanation: Look for 猫走到一棵树 — it directly confirms walked under a tree and sat down.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The strong sun drives everyone indoors and even the cat seeks shade — the weather shapes the whole scene.

title: The Elevator Conversation
text_zh: 我住在十楼。每天早上坐电梯下去。电梯里的人都不说话。今天有一个小孩对我说「你好！」我说「你好。」他妈妈笑了。那天早上，我很高兴。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài shí lóu. Měi tiān zǎoshang zuò diàntī xià qù. Diàntī lǐ de rén dōu bù shuō huà. Jīntiān yǒu yí gè xiǎohái duì wǒ shuō「nǐ hǎo!」Wǒ shuō「nǐ hǎo.」Tā māma xiào le. Nà tiān zǎoshang, wǒ hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: I live on the tenth floor. Every morning I take the elevator down. People in the elevator don't talk. Today a child said 'Hello!' to me. I said 'Hello.' His mom smiled. That morning, I was happy.

questions.q_en: What floor does 'I' live on?
questions.explanation: Look for 我住在十楼 (tenth floor) in the opening line — I live on the tenth floor.

questions.q_zh: 电梯里的人平时怎么样？
questions.q_en: How are the people in the elevator usually?
questions.explanation: Look for 电梯里 (all silent) in the third sentence — people in the elevator don't talk.

questions.q_en: Who said 'hello' first?
questions.explanation: Look for 小孩对我说 (the child) in the middle of the passage — a child said hello first.

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 医院里有很多人在等。一个老人在看报纸。一个妈妈带着小孩，小孩在玩手机。我等了四十分钟了。护士出来叫了一个名字，不是我的。大家继续等。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn lǐ yǒu hěn duō rén zài děng. Yí gè lǎorén zài kàn bàozhǐ. Yí gè māma dàizhe xiǎohái, xiǎohái zài wán shǒujī. Wǒ děng le sìshí fēnzhōng le. Hùshi chūlái jiào le yí gè míngzi, bú shì wǒ de. Dàjiā jìxù děng.
text_en: Many people are waiting in the hospital. An old man is reading a newspaper. A mother is with a child; the child is playing with a phone. I've waited forty minutes. A nurse came out and called a name — not mine. Everyone keeps waiting.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: The old man reads a newspaper, the child plays with a phone — small details show how naturally people occupy waiting time.

questions.q_en: Did the nurse call 'me'?
questions.explanation: Look for 不 (no) in the passage — the nurse called a name.

questions.q_en: How long has 'I' waited?
questions.explanation: Look for 我等了四十分 (forty minutes) in the middle of the passage — I've waited forty minutes.

title: The New Teacher
text_zh: 今天学校来了一个新老师。她很年轻，说话的声音很小。学生们都很安静地听。下课以后，一个学生说「我喜欢这个老师，她说的话我都听得懂。」
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xuéxiào lái le yí gè xīn lǎoshī. Tā hěn niánqīng, shuō huà de shēngyīn hěn xiǎo. Xuéshēngmen dōu hěn ānjìng de tīng. Xià kè yǐhòu, yí gè xuéshēng shuō「wǒ xǐhuan zhège lǎoshī, tā shuō de huà wǒ dōu tīng de dǒng.」
text_en: Today a new teacher came to school. She's young and speaks softly. The students all listened quietly. After class, one student said 'I like this teacher — I can understand everything she says.'

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: A student says they can understand everything — the new teacher's clarity brings a shift in how students experience learning.

questions.q_zh: 学生们上课的时候怎么样？
questions.q_en: How were the students during class?
questions.explanation: Look for 安静地听 (very quiet) in the third sentence — the students all listened quietly.

questions.q_zh: 那个学生为什么喜欢这个老师？
questions.q_en: Why does the student like this teacher?

options.pinyin: tā shuō de huà dōu tīng de dǒng

questions.explanation: The student says I can understand everything she says — that's why they like her.

title: The Library Rule
text_zh: 图书馆里不能说话。一个男人在打电话，声音很大。工作的人走过去对他说「请到外面打电话。」他说「对不起」，就出去了。图书馆又安静了。
text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn lǐ bù néng shuō huà. Yí gè nánrén zài dǎ diànhuà, shēngyīn hěn dà. Gōngzuò de rén zǒu guòqù duì tā shuō「qǐng dào wàimiàn dǎ diànhuà.」Tā shuō「duìbuqǐ」, jiù chūqù le. Túshūguǎn yòu ānjìng le.
text_en: You can't talk in the library. A man was making a phone call, very loudly. A staff member walked over and said 'Please go outside to make your call.' He said 'Sorry' and went out. The library was quiet again.

questions.q_en: Can you talk in the library?
questions.explanation: Look for 不能 (no) in the opening line — you can't talk in the library.

questions.q_en: What was the man doing?
questions.explanation: Look for 打电话 (making a phone call) in the second sentence — a man was making a phone call, very loudly.

questions.q_en: What did he do in the end?
questions.explanation: Look for 出去了 (went outside) in the passage — he said sorry and went outside to finish his call.

title: The School Cafeteria
text_zh: 学校的食堂十一点半开门。十一点二十，门口已经有人在等了。我每天都十二点去，所以每天都要等。今天我十一点半就去了。前面只有三个人。我觉得很好。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào de shítáng shíyī diǎn bàn kāimén. Shíyī diǎn èrshí, ménkǒu yǐjīng yǒu rén zài děng le. Wǒ měi tiān dōu shí èr diǎn qù, suǒyǐ měi tiān dōu yào děng. Jīntiān wǒ shíyī diǎn bàn jiù qù le. Qiánmiàn zhǐ yǒu sān gè rén. Wǒ juéde hěn hǎo.
text_en: The school cafeteria opens at 11:30. At 11:20, people are already waiting at the door. I usually go at twelve, so I always have to wait. Today I went at 11:30. Only three people were ahead of me. I thought that was great.

questions.q_en: What time does the cafeteria open?
questions.explanation: Look for 学校的食堂十 (eleven thirty) in the opening line — the school cafeteria opens at 11:30.

questions.q_en: What time does 'I' usually go?
questions.explanation: Look for 学校的食堂十 (twelve) in the third sentence — I usually go at twelve.

questions.q_zh: 今天「我」去得早，前面有几个人？
questions.q_en: Today 'I' went early — how many people were ahead?
questions.explanation: Look for 三个 (three) in the passage — only three people were ahead of me.

title: The Post Office Clock
text_zh: 我们这里的邮局有一个很大的钟。这个钟很老了，但是每天都很准。很多人走路的时候会看一下这个钟。我爸爸说「这个钟比我还老。」我觉得很有意思。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen zhèlǐ de yóujú yǒu yí gè hěn dà de zhōng. Zhège zhōng hěn lǎo le, dànshì měi tiān dōu hěn zhǔn. Hěn duō rén zǒulù de shíhou huì kàn yíxià zhège zhōng. Wǒ bàba shuō「zhège zhōng bǐ wǒ hái lǎo.」Wǒ juéde hěn yǒu yìsi.
text_en: The post office here has a big clock. The clock is old, but it's always accurate. Many people look at the clock when they walk by. My dad said 'This clock is even older than me.' I thought that was funny.

questions.q_zh: 这段话会让不同的人有不同的理解吗？
questions.q_en: Would different people interpret this passage differently?

options.text: 是的，因为涉及个人经历
options.pinyin: shì de, yīnwèi shèjí gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: yes, because it involves personal experience

options.pinyin: zhǐ yǒu zhuānjiā cái néng lǐjiě

questions.explanation: Dad's remark about the clock's age is funny or serious depending on who hears it — personal experience shapes the reading.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: The old clock is a daily fixture — if it stopped or disappeared, people who rely on it would feel off-balance.

questions.q_en: What did dad say?

options.text_en: the clock is older than him

questions.explanation: Look for 钟比他还老 (the clock is older than him) — dad said this clock is even older than me.

title: The Bank at Closing Time
text_zh: 银行四点半关门。现在四点二十了。一个女人跑进来，说「还能办吗？」工作的人说「能，快一点。」她坐下来，很快办好了。她说「谢谢你们，差一点就来不了了。」
text_pinyin: Yínháng sì diǎn bàn guānmén. Xiànzài sì diǎn èrshí le. Yí gè nǚrén pǎo jìnlái, shuō「hái néng bàn ma?」Gōngzuò de rén shuō「néng, kuài yìdiǎn.」Tā zuò xiàlái, hěn kuài bàn hǎo le. Tā shuō「xièxie nǐmen, chà yìdiǎn jiù lái bù liǎo le.」
text_en: The bank closes at 4:30. It's now 4:20. A woman ran in and said 'Can I still do it?' The staff said 'Yes, quickly.' She sat down and finished fast. She said 'Thank you — I almost didn't make it.'

questions.q_en: What time does the bank close?
questions.explanation: Look for 银行四点半关 (four thirty) in the opening line — the bank closes at 4:30.

questions.q_zh: 她来的时候还有多长时间？
questions.q_en: How much time was left when she arrived?
questions.explanation: She arrived at 4:20 and the bank closes at 4:30 — ten minutes left.

questions.q_en: Did the staff let her do it?
questions.explanation: Look for 让了 (yes) — the staff said yes, quickly — they let her do.

title: My Name in Two Languages
text_zh: 我有两个名字。一个是中文名字，一个是英文名字。在家里，妈妈叫我的中文名字。在学校，老师叫我的英文名字。有时候我不知道哪个才是「真的我」。两个都是吧。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Yí gè shì Zhōngwén míngzi, yí gè shì Yīngwén míngzi. Zài jiā lǐ, māma jiào wǒ de Zhōngwén míngzi. Zài xuéxiào, lǎoshī jiào wǒ de Yīngwén míngzi. Yǒu shíhou wǒ bù zhīdào nǎ ge cái shì「zhēn de wǒ」. Liǎng gè dōu shì ba.
text_en: I have two names. One is a Chinese name, one is an English name. At home, mom calls my Chinese name. At school, teachers call my English name. Sometimes I don't know which one is the 'real me.' Both of them, I suppose.

questions.q_en: Who uses the Chinese name at home?
questions.explanation: Look for 妈妈 (mom) in the third sentence — at home, mom calls my Chinese name.

questions.q_zh: 说话的方式反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the way of speaking reflect?

options.pinyin: shuōhuà rén de xìnggé hé tàidu

questions.explanation: Having two names used in different contexts — the split itself reveals something about how identity shifts with language.

questions.q_zh: 对话中没有说出来的是什么？
questions.q_en: What is left unsaid in the conversation?
questions.explanation: The narrator doesn't know which name is the real me — there are deeper feelings about identity left unspoken.

title: What My Hands Know
text_zh: 我的手会做很多事。会写中文字，会做饭，会开车。但是我的手不会弹钢琴。我妈妈的手会。我看她弹的时候，觉得她的手和我的手不一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de shǒu huì zuò hěn duō shì. Huì xiě Zhōngwén zì, huì zuò fàn, huì kāi chē. Dànshì wǒ de shǒu bú huì tán gāngqín. Wǒ māma de shǒu huì. Wǒ kàn tā tán de shíhou, juéde tā de shǒu hé wǒ de shǒu bù yíyàng.
text_en: My hands can do many things. They can write Chinese characters, cook, and drive. But my hands can't play piano. My mom's hands can. When I watch her play, I feel that her hands and mine are different.

questions.q_en: What can't 'my' hands do?
questions.explanation: Look for 手不会弹钢琴 (play piano) in the third sentence — my hands can't play piano.

questions.q_en: Who can play piano?
questions.explanation: Look for 妈妈 (mom) in the middle of the passage — mom's hands can play piano.

questions.q_zh: 「我」看妈妈弹琴的时候觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel watching mom play?

options.text: 觉得她的手和自己的不一样
options.pinyin: juéde tā de shǒu hé zìjǐ de bù yíyàng

questions.explanation: The clue is 觉得她的手和 — watching mom play, I feel that her hands and mine are different.

title: The Photo from Before
text_zh: 我看到了一张十年以前的照片。照片里的人是我，但是我觉得不太像。那个时候我很瘦，头发很长。现在我不一样了。但是笑的样子，还是一样的。
text_pinyin: Wǒ kàn dào le yì zhāng shí nián yǐqián de zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn lǐ de rén shì wǒ, dànshì wǒ juéde bú tài xiàng. Nà ge shíhou wǒ hěn shòu, tóufa hěn cháng. Xiànzài wǒ bù yíyàng le. Dànshì xiào de yàngzi, háishi yíyàng de.
text_en: I saw a photo from ten years ago. The person in the photo is me, but I feel it doesn't quite look like me. Back then I was thin, with long hair. Now I'm different. But the way I smile — that's still the same.

questions.q_zh: 人物的行为暗示了什么内心想法？
questions.q_en: What inner thoughts does the character's behavior hint at?
questions.explanation: Looking at an old photo and feeling changed — the narrator is searching for peace and meaning in who they've become.

questions.q_en: What was 'I' like before?
questions.explanation: Look for 很瘦头发长 (thin, long hair) — back then I was thin, with long hair.

questions.q_en: What hasn't changed?
questions.explanation: Look for 笑的样子 (the way of smiling) in the closing line — the way I smile — that's still the same.

title: Speaking Chinese at the Store
text_zh: 我在一个中国商店买东西。我用中文说「这个多少钱？」那个人很高兴，用中文回答我。我又说了几句，他说「你的中文很好！」我知道还不太好，但是我很高兴能用中文买东西。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài yí gè Zhōngguó shāngdiàn mǎi dōngxi. Wǒ yòng Zhōngwén shuō「zhège duōshǎo qián?」Nà ge rén hěn gāoxìng, yòng Zhōngwén huídá wǒ. Wǒ yòu shuō le jǐ jù, tā shuō「nǐ de Zhōngwén hěn hǎo!」Wǒ zhīdào hái bú tài hǎo, dànshì wǒ hěn gāoxìng néng yòng Zhōngwén mǎi dōngxi.
text_en: I was buying things at a Chinese shop. I said in Chinese 'How much is this?' The person was happy and answered me in Chinese. I said a few more things, and he said 'Your Chinese is great!' I know it's not that good yet, but I was happy to be able to buy things in Chinese.

questions.q_en: What language did 'I' use to buy things?
questions.explanation: Look for 中文 (Chinese) in the second sentence — I said in chinese: how much is this?

questions.q_zh: 那个人说「我」的中文怎么样？
questions.q_en: What did the person say about 'my' Chinese?
questions.explanation: Look for 很好 (very good) in the passage — the person said: your Chinese is great.

questions.q_en: Does 'I' think their own Chinese is good?
questions.explanation: Look for 我知道还不太 (not that good yet) in the final sentence — I know it's not that good yet.

title: Dad's Noodles
text_zh: 我爸爸只会做一个菜——面条。但是他做的面条很好吃。他每个星期天都做。我们一家人坐在一起吃。我妈妈说「别的你都不会做，就这个好。」爸爸笑了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ bàba zhǐ huì zuò yí gè cài——miàntiáo. Dànshì tā zuò de miàntiáo hěn hǎochī. Tā měi ge xīngqītiān dōu zuò. Wǒmen yì jiā rén zuò zài yìqǐ chī. Wǒ māma shuō「bié de nǐ dōu bú huì zuò, jiù zhège hǎo.」Bàba xiào le.
text_en: My dad can only make one dish — noodles. But his noodles are delicious. He makes them every Sunday. Our whole family sits together and eats. My mom said 'You can't make anything else, just this one thing is good.' Dad smiled.

questions.q_zh: 文中哪个细节最能体现作者的态度？
questions.q_en: Which detail best reflects the author's attitude?
questions.explanation: The passage focuses on dad's one dish and the family sitting together — finding meaning in a simple, repeated moment.

questions.q_zh: 如果把这段话和新闻报道比较，最大的区别是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the biggest difference between this passage and a news report?
questions.explanation: The passage centers on how noodles taste and how the family feels — emotion matters more than a recipe.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: Family eating together, mom teasing dad gently, dad smiling — the feeling is warm throughout.

title: Tea with Grandma
text_zh: 每次去奶奶家，她都会给我泡茶。她说「先坐下，喝杯茶。」我不太喜欢喝茶，但是在奶奶家我总是喝。因为喝茶的时候，我们可以慢慢说话。这个时间我很喜欢。
text_pinyin: Měi cì qù nǎinai jiā, tā dōu huì gěi wǒ pào chá. Tā shuō「xiān zuò xià, hē bēi chá.」Wǒ bú tài xǐhuan hē chá, dànshì zài nǎinai jiā wǒ zǒngshì hē. Yīnwèi hē chá de shíhou, wǒmen kěyǐ mànmàn shuō huà. Zhège shíjiān wǒ hěn xǐhuan.
text_en: Every time I go to grandma's house, she makes me tea. She says 'Sit down first, have a cup of tea.' I don't like tea, but at grandma's house I always drink it. Because when we drink tea, we can talk slowly. I like that time.

questions.q_en: Does 'I' like drinking tea?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 太喜欢喝茶， — I don't like tea, but at grandma's house I always drink.

questions.q_zh: 为什么「我」在奶奶家总是喝茶？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' always drink tea at grandma's?
questions.explanation: Look for 因为喝茶的时 — when we drink tea, we can talk slowly — that's why the narrator drinks.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: Always drinking tea at grandma's despite not liking it — the ritual of talking together clearly matters deeply.

title: The Restaurant with One Table
text_zh: 这个饭馆很小，只有一张大桌子。不认识的人也坐在一起吃饭。今天我旁边坐了一个老人。他点了鱼。我点了面条。他看了我的面条说「看起来不错。」我说「你的鱼也很好。」
text_pinyin: Zhège fànguǎn hěn xiǎo, zhǐ yǒu yì zhāng dà zhuōzi. Bú rènshi de rén yě zuò zài yìqǐ chī fàn. Jīntiān wǒ pángbiān zuò le yí gè lǎorén. Tā diǎn le yú. Wǒ diǎn le miàntiáo. Tā kàn le wǒ de miàntiáo shuō「kàn qǐlái búcuò.」Wǒ shuō「nǐ de yú yě hěn hǎo.」
text_en: This restaurant is small — just one big table. Strangers sit together to eat. Today an old man sat next to me. He ordered fish. I ordered noodles. He looked at my noodles and said 'That looks good.' I said 'Your fish looks good too.'

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: They just met at a shared table and comment on each other's food — the small exchange shows quick, easy familiarity.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' order?
questions.explanation: Look for 面条 (noodles) in the passage — I ordered noodles.

questions.q_en: Do the people sitting together know each other?
questions.explanation: Look for 不认识 (no) in the second sentence — strangers sit together — they don't know each other.

title: The Last Dumpling
text_zh: 桌子上还有一个饺子。我想吃，我朋友也想吃。我们看了看对方，都笑了。我说「你吃吧。」他说「不，你吃。」最后我们把饺子分成了两半，一人一半。
text_pinyin: Zhuōzi shang hái yǒu yí gè jiǎozi. Wǒ xiǎng chī, wǒ péngyou yě xiǎng chī. Wǒmen kàn le kàn duìfāng, dōu xiào le. Wǒ shuō「nǐ chī ba.」Tā shuō「bù, nǐ chī.」Zuìhòu wǒmen bǎ jiǎozi fēn chéng le liǎng bàn, yì rén yí bàn.
text_en: There's one dumpling left on the table. I want to eat it, and my friend does too. We looked at each other and both laughed. I said 'You eat it.' He said 'No, you eat it.' In the end we split the dumpling in two — half each.

questions.q_zh: 这段话让你想到了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this passage make you think of?
questions.explanation: Two friends fighting over who gets the last dumpling — most people have had a similar lighthearted moment.

questions.q_en: What did they do in the end?
questions.explanation: Look for 分成两半 (split it in half) — they split the dumpling in two — half each.

questions.q_zh: 他们看对方的时候做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did they do when they looked at each other?
questions.explanation: Look for 笑了 (laughed) in the third sentence — they looked at each other and both laughed.

title: The Missing Cup
text_zh: 早上我的杯子在桌子上。中午回来，杯子不见了。我问了同事，他们都说不知道。下午三点，我在厨房找到了。杯子里还有茶。谁喝了我的茶？没有人说。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang wǒ de bēizi zài zhuōzi shang. Zhōngwǔ huílái, bēizi bújiàn le. Wǒ wèn le tóngshì, tāmen dōu shuō bù zhīdào. Xiàwǔ sān diǎn, wǒ zài chúfáng zhǎo dào le. Bēizi lǐ hái yǒu chá. Shéi hē le wǒ de chá? Méiyǒu rén shuō.
text_en: In the morning my cup was on the table. I came back at noon and the cup was gone. I asked my colleagues — they all said they didn't know. At three in the afternoon, I found it in the kitchen. There was still tea in the cup. Who drank my tea? Nobody said.

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: Someone drank the tea and nobody admits it — the lingering mystery stirs a familiar feeling of puzzled suspicion.

questions.q_zh: 同事们知道杯子在哪里吗？
questions.q_en: Did the colleagues know where the cup was?
questions.explanation: Look for 都说不知道 (all said no) in the third sentence — the colleagues all said they didn't know.

questions.q_en: What was still in the cup?
questions.explanation: Look for 茶 (tea) in the passage — there was still tea in the cup.

title: Who Opened the Window?
text_zh: 我出门的时候，窗户是关着的。回来以后，窗户开了。家里没有别的人。猫在窗户旁边坐着，看着外面。我看了看猫，猫看了看我。我觉得我知道是谁了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ chūmén de shíhou, chuānghu shì guānzhe de. Huílái yǐhòu, chuānghu kāi le. Jiā lǐ méiyǒu bié de rén. Māo zài chuānghu pángbiān zuòzhe, kànzhe wàimiàn. Wǒ kàn le kàn māo, māo kàn le kàn wǒ. Wǒ juéde wǒ zhīdào shì shéi le.
text_en: When I left, the window was closed. When I came back, the window was open. No one else was home. The cat was sitting by the window, looking outside. I looked at the cat, the cat looked at me. I think I know who did it.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: A closed window now open, a cat sitting by it — the small mystery creates a mood of quiet intrigue.

questions.q_en: Who does 'I' think opened the window?
questions.explanation: Look for 猫 (the cat) in the passage — I think I know who did it — the cat is sitting by the open window.

questions.q_en: Was anyone else at home?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有别的人 (no) in the third sentence — no one else was home.

title: The Wrong Number
text_zh: 晚上九点，我的电话响了。一个女人说「小王，你在哪里？我等你很久了。」我说「对不起，你打错了。我不是小王。」她说「啊，对不起！」然后挂了。我想，小王去哪里了？
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang jiǔ diǎn, wǒ de diànhuà xiǎng le. Yí gè nǚrén shuō「Xiǎo Wáng, nǐ zài nǎlǐ? Wǒ děng nǐ hěn jiǔ le.」Wǒ shuō「duìbuqǐ, nǐ dǎ cuò le. Wǒ bú shì Xiǎo Wáng.」Tā shuō「à, duìbuqǐ!」Ránhòu guà le. Wǒ xiǎng, Xiǎo Wáng qù nǎlǐ le?
text_en: At nine at night, my phone rang. A woman said 'Xiao Wang, where are you? I've been waiting a long time.' I said 'Sorry, you have the wrong number. I'm not Xiao Wang.' She said 'Oh, sorry!' Then hung up. I wondered — where did Xiao Wang go?

questions.q_en: Who was the woman looking for?
questions.explanation: Look for 小王 (Xiao Wang) in the second sentence — the woman was looking for xiao wang.

questions.q_en: What time was the call?
questions.explanation: Look for 九点 (nine) in the opening line — the phone rang at nine at night.

questions.q_en: What was 'I' thinking about at the end?
questions.explanation: Look for 小王去哪里了 (where Xiao Wang went) in the closing line — the narrator wondered: where did xiao wang go?

title: The Chair That Moved
text_zh: 我的椅子早上在桌子左边。下午回来，椅子在桌子右边了。我问妈妈「你动了我的椅子吗？」她说「没有。」我问弟弟，他也说「没有。」谁动了我的椅子？到现在我也不知道。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de yǐzi zǎoshang zài zhuōzi zuǒbiān. Xiàwǔ huílái, yǐzi zài zhuōzi yòubiān le. Wǒ wèn māma「nǐ dòng le wǒ de yǐzi ma?」Tā shuō「méiyǒu.」Wǒ wèn dìdi, tā yě shuō「méiyǒu.」Shéi dòng le wǒ de yǐzi? Dào xiànzài wǒ yě bù zhīdào.
text_en: My chair was on the left side of the table in the morning. When I came back in the afternoon, it was on the right side. I asked mom 'Did you move my chair?' She said 'No.' I asked my brother — he also said 'No.' Who moved my chair? I still don't know.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: Asking mom and brother about a moved chair — the easy back-and-forth shows a close family relationship.

questions.q_en: Who did 'I' ask?
questions.explanation: Look for 妈妈和弟弟 (mom and brother) — I asked mom and then asked my brother.

questions.q_en: Did they find out who moved it?
questions.explanation: Look for 不知道 (don't know) in the closing line — I still don't know who moved the chair.

title: The Note in the Book
text_zh: 我在图书馆借了一本书。回家打开看的时候，里面有一张小纸条。上面写着「这本书很好，希望你也喜欢。」没有名字。我不知道是谁写的，但是我觉得很高兴。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài túshūguǎn jiè le yì běn shū. Huí jiā dǎkāi kàn de shíhou, lǐmiàn yǒu yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo. Shàngmiàn xiězhe「zhè běn shū hěn hǎo, xīwàng nǐ yě xǐhuan.」Méiyǒu míngzi. Wǒ bù zhīdào shì shéi xiě de, dànshì wǒ juéde hěn gāoxìng.
text_en: I borrowed a book from the library. When I opened it at home, there was a small note inside. It said 'This book is good, I hope you like it too.' No name. I don't know who wrote it, but I felt happy.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: Finding a kind anonymous note in a library book — the author wants you to feel that same small, unexpected happiness.

questions.q_en: Was there a name on the note?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有 (no) in the passage — there was no name on the note.

questions.q_zh: 「我」看到纸条以后觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after seeing the note?
questions.explanation: The clue is 很高兴 (very happy) in the closing line — I felt happy after seeing the note.

title: Dad's New Phone
text_zh: 爸爸买了一个新手机。他不太会用。他想给我打电话，但是打给了妈妈。他想看天气，但是打开了照相机。他把手机放在桌子上，对它说「你太难了！」
text_pinyin: Bàba mǎi le yí gè xīn shǒujī. Tā bú tài huì yòng. Tā xiǎng gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà, dànshì dǎ gěi le māma. Tā xiǎng kàn tiānqì, dànshì dǎkāi le zhàoxiàngjī. Tā duì shǒujī shuō「nǐ tài nán le!」Shǒujī méiyǒu huídá tā.
text_en: Dad bought a new phone. He doesn't know how to use it. He wanted to call me but called mom instead. He wanted to check the weather but opened the camera. He put the phone on the table and said to it 'You're too hard!'

questions.q_en: Who did dad want to call?
questions.explanation: Look for 我 (me) in the third sentence — he wanted to call me but called mom instead.

questions.q_zh: 他想看天气，打开了什么？
questions.q_en: He wanted to check the weather — what did he open?
questions.explanation: Look for 照相机 (camera) in the passage — he wanted to check the weather but opened the camera.

questions.q_en: Did the phone answer dad?
questions.explanation: The phone is an object — it can't answer. Dad talked to it out of frustration, not expecting a reply.

title: The Dog's Enemy
text_zh: 我家的狗看到了镜子里的另一只狗。它叫了很久。镜子里的狗也叫了很久——其实没有声音，但是嘴在动。我家的狗更生气了：你叫什么叫？它跑到镜子后面。后面什么都没有。它又跑回来——那只狗还在！它看了看我，好像在说「你怎么不帮我？」。最后它不高兴地走了。走了三步，又回头看了一眼。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā de gǒu kàn dào le jìngzi lǐ de lìng yì zhī gǒu. Tā jiào le hěn jiǔ. Jìngzi lǐ de gǒu yě jiào le hěn jiǔ — qíshí méiyǒu shēngyīn, dànshì zuǐ zài dòng. Wǒ jiā de gǒu gèng shēngqì le: nǐ jiào shénme jiào? Tā pǎo dào jìngzi hòumiàn. Hòumiàn shénme dōu méiyǒu. Tā yòu pǎo huílái — nà zhī gǒu hái zài! Tā kàn le kàn wǒ, hǎoxiàng zài shuō 'nǐ zěnme bù bāng wǒ?'. Zuìhòu tā bù gāoxìng de zǒu le. Zǒu le sān bù, yòu huítóu kàn le yì yǎn.
text_en: My dog spotted another dog in the mirror. It barked for a long time. The mirror dog barked back — no sound, actually, but its mouth was moving. My dog got angrier: what are you barking at? It ran behind the mirror. Nothing back there. It ran back around — that dog was still there! It looked at me like: why aren't you helping? Finally it walked away, unhappy. Three steps later, it looked back one more time.

questions.q_zh: 狗知道镜子里的是自己吗？
questions.q_en: Did the dog know it was itself in the mirror?
questions.explanation: The dog barked at its own reflection, ran behind the mirror, and came back confused — it didn't recognize itself.

questions.q_zh: 狗跑到镜子后面看到了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the dog see behind the mirror?
questions.explanation: Look for 什么都没有 (nothing) in the passage — the dog ran behind.

questions.q_en: How did the dog end up?
questions.explanation: Look for 不高兴地走了 (walked away unhappily) in the passage — the dog walked away unhappily, looking back one more time.

title: The Serious Reader
text_zh: 咖啡店里，一个男人在看书。他看了很久，表情很认真。旁边的人看了看他的书，说「你的书拿反了」。那个男人低头看了看。真的反了。他笑了，说「啊，我在练习」。旁边的人没有说话。
text_pinyin: Kāfēi diàn lǐ, yí gè nánrén zài kàn shū. Tā kàn le hěn jiǔ, biǎoqíng hěn rènzhēn. Pángbiān de rén kàn le kàn tā de shū, shuō 'nǐ de shū ná fǎn le'. Nà ge nánrén dītóu kàn le kàn. Zhēn de fǎn le. Tā xiào le, shuō 'a, wǒ zài liànxí'. Pángbiān de rén méiyǒu shuō huà.
text_en: In a coffee shop, a man was reading. He'd been at it a while, expression very serious. The person next to him glanced at his book and said 'Your book is upside down.' The man looked down. It really was. He laughed and said 'Oh, I'm practicing.' The person next to him said nothing.

questions.q_en: What was wrong with the book?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 旁边的人看 — the person next to him pointed out: your book is upside down.

questions.q_en: Who told him?
questions.explanation: Look for 旁边的人 (the person next to him) in the third sentence — the person next to him told him.

questions.q_zh: 那个男人的反应是什么？
questions.q_en: What was the man's reaction?
questions.explanation: Look for 笑了 (laughed) in the passage — he laughed and said he was 'practicing.'.

title: Grandpa's Glasses
text_zh: 爷爷找不到他的眼镜了。他问奶奶「你看到我的眼镜了吗？」奶奶看了看他，笑了。她说「在你的头上啊。」爷爷用手摸了摸头。眼镜果然在那里。
text_pinyin: Yéye zhǎo bú dào tā de yǎnjìng le. Tā wèn nǎinai「nǐ kàn dào wǒ de yǎnjìng le ma?」Nǎinai kàn le kàn tā, xiào le. Tā shuō「zài nǐ de tóu shang a.」Yéye yòng shǒu mō le mō tóu, yǎnjìng guǒrán zài nàlǐ. Tā yě xiào le.
text_en: Grandpa can't find his glasses. He asked grandma 'Have you seen my glasses?' Grandma looked at him and laughed. She said 'They're on your head.' Grandpa reached up and felt his head. The glasses were right there.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: Grandpa searching for glasses that are on his head, grandma laughing — it's a gentle, affectionate family moment.

questions.q_en: Who did grandpa ask?
questions.explanation: Look for 奶奶 (grandma) in the second sentence — he asked grandma.

questions.q_en: Why did grandma laugh?

options.pinyin: yǎnjìng jiù zài yéye tóu shang
options.text_en: glasses were right on grandpa's head

questions.explanation: The clue is 眼镜就在爷 (glasses were right on grandpa's head) — the glasses were on grandpa's head the whole time.

title: The Cat's Favorite Chair
text_zh: 我家有三把椅子。猫最喜欢我的那把。每次我站起来，猫就跳上去坐。我回来的时候，猫不走。我看着猫，猫闭着眼睛。我只好坐别的椅子。这把椅子现在是猫的了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā yǒu sān bǎ yǐzi. Māo zuì xǐhuan wǒ de nà bǎ. Měi cì wǒ zhàn qǐlái, māo jiù tiào shàngqù zuò. Wǒ huílái de shíhou, māo bù zǒu. Wǒ kànzhe māo, māo bìzhe yǎnjing. Wǒ zhǐhǎo zuò bié de yǐzi. Zhè bǎ yǐzi xiànzài shì māo de le.
text_en: Our house has three chairs. The cat likes mine best. Every time I stand up, the cat jumps on it and sits. When I come back, the cat won't move. I look at the cat — the cat has its eyes closed. I have no choice but to sit in another chair. This chair belongs to the cat now.

questions.q_zh: 文中哪个细节最能体现作者的态度？
questions.q_en: Which detail best reflects the author's attitude?
questions.explanation: The narrator closely tracks which chair the cat takes and how it behaves — focused on small, everyday things.

questions.q_zh: 观察的行为说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the act of observing suggest?
questions.explanation: The narrator watches the cat claim the chair, eyes closed, refusing to move — noticing these small behaviors closely.

questions.q_zh: 安静的场景传达了什么信息？
questions.q_en: What does the quiet scene communicate?
questions.explanation: The cat claims the chair, the narrator accepts it — there's a calm surrender and peace in this little routine.

title: The First Egg Fried Rice
text_zh: 我想做鸡蛋炒饭。我看了手机上的视频三次。视频说「一点点油」。我放了很多。视频说「一个鸡蛋」。我的鸡蛋太大了，锅太小了，油到处都是。我很紧张。饭放进去的时候，锅里发出了很大的声音。我用了二十分钟。做完以后，我看了看——颜色不太对，形状也不太好看。但是我吃了一口。还可以。
text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng zuò jīdàn chǎofàn. Wǒ kàn le shǒujī shàng de shìpín sān cì. Shìpín shuō 'yìdiǎndiǎn yóu'. Wǒ fàng le hěn duō. Shìpín shuō 'yí gè jīdàn'. Wǒ de jīdàn tài dà le, guō tài xiǎo le, yóu dàochù dōu shì. Wǒ hěn jǐnzhāng. Fàn fàng jìnqù de shíhou, guō lǐ fāchū le hěn dà de shēngyīn. Wǒ yòng le èrshí fēnzhōng. Zuò wán yǐhòu, wǒ kàn le kàn — yánsè bú tài duì, xíngzhuàng yě bú tài hǎokàn. Dànshì wǒ chī le yì kǒu. Hái kěyǐ.
text_en: I wanted to make egg fried rice. I watched the phone video three times. The video said 'a little oil.' I put in a lot. The video said 'one egg.' My egg was too big, the pan too small, oil everywhere. I was nervous. When the rice went in, the pan made a loud noise. It took me twenty minutes. When I finished, I looked at it — wrong color, wrong shape. But I took a bite. Not bad.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' want to make?
questions.explanation: Look for 鸡蛋炒饭 (egg fried rice) in the opening line — I wanted to make egg fried rice.

questions.q_zh: 做饭的时候出了什么问题？
questions.q_en: What went wrong while cooking?

options.text: 油多了，鸡蛋大了，锅小了
options.pinyin: yóu duō le, jīdàn dà le, guō xiǎo le
options.text_en: too much oil, egg too big, pan too small

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 我的鸡蛋太大 — too much oil, the egg was too big, and the pan was too small.

questions.q_en: How did it taste in the end?
questions.explanation: Look for 还可以 (not bad) in the closing line — I took a bite and it was not bad.

title: The Airport at Five AM
text_zh: 早上五点，机场已经有很多人了。有的人在睡觉，用包当枕头。有的人在喝咖啡，看手机。我坐在窗户旁边，看外面的飞机。天还没亮。我的飞机七点走。还有两个小时。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang wǔ diǎn, jīchǎng yǐjīng yǒu hěn duō rén le. Yǒu de rén zài shuìjiào, yòng bāo dāng zhěntou. Yǒu de rén zài hē kāfēi, kàn shǒujī. Wǒ zuò zài chuānghu pángbiān, kàn wàimiàn de fēijī. Tiān hái méi liàng. Wǒ de fēijī qī diǎn zǒu. Hái yǒu liǎng gè xiǎoshí.
text_en: At five in the morning, the airport already has many people. Some are sleeping, using bags as pillows. Some are drinking coffee and looking at phones. I sit by the window, watching the planes outside. It's not light yet. My plane leaves at seven. Two more hours.

questions.q_en: What time is it now?
questions.explanation: Look for 五点 (five) in the opening line — at five in the morning — that's the current time.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: It's dark outside, people are sleeping on bags, drinking coffee — the pre-dawn airport setting creates a specific mood.

questions.q_en: What time does the plane leave?
questions.explanation: Look for 我的飞机七点 (seven) in the middle of the passage — my plane leaves at seven.

title: The Hotel Room Number
text_zh: 我的酒店房间是八零三。我记得很清楚。但是晚上回来的时候，我走到了八零二的门口。我用房卡开不了门。我看了看门上的号——八零二。我的房间在旁边。太累了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de jiǔdiàn fángjiān shì bā líng sān. Wǒ jìde hěn qīngchu. Dànshì wǎnshang huílái de shíhou, wǒ zǒu dào le bā líng èr de ménkǒu. Wǒ yòng fángkǎ kāi bù liǎo mén. Wǒ kàn le kàn mén shang de hào——bā líng èr. Wǒ de fángjiān zài pángbiān. Tài lèi le.
text_en: My hotel room is 803. I remember clearly. But when I came back at night, I went to room 802's door. My room card couldn't open it. I looked at the number on the door — 802. My room is next door. Too tired.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: Going to the wrong hotel room because of tiredness — a small detail, but it says something about the narrator's state.

questions.q_en: Why couldn't the door open?
questions.explanation: Look for 酒店房间是八 — my room card couldn't open it because the narrator went to room 802 instead of 803.

questions.q_en: Why did 'I' go to the wrong room?
questions.explanation: Look for 太累了 — 'too tired' — that's why the narrator went to the wrong door.

title: The Train Station Announcement
text_zh: 火车站的广播说「去北京的火车快要开了。」我听到了，但是我不去北京。我去上海。我的火车还有一个小时。我坐在椅子上，看着去北京的人都跑起来了。不着急，我的还早。
text_pinyin: Huǒchēzhàn de guǎngbō shuō「qù Běijīng de huǒchē kuàiyào kāi le.」Wǒ tīng dào le, dànshì wǒ bú qù Běijīng. Wǒ qù Shànghǎi. Wǒ de huǒchē hái yǒu yí gè xiǎoshí. Wǒ zuò zài yǐzi shang, kànzhe qù Běijīng de rén dōu pǎo qǐlái le. Bù zháojí, wǒ de hái zǎo.
text_en: The train station announcement says 'The train to Beijing is about to depart.' I heard it, but I'm not going to Beijing. I'm going to Shanghai. My train is in one hour. I sit in my chair, watching all the people going to Beijing start running. No rush — mine is still a while away.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The narrator is calm and unhurried watching others run — the relaxed tone reflects an easy, settled mood.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: Sitting calmly while others rush around creates a contrast — the quiet observation sets an atmosphere.

questions.q_en: Is 'I' in a rush?
questions.explanation: Look for 不着急 (not rushed) in the closing line — the narrator says no rush, their train is still a while away.

title: Why We Say 'Hello'
text_zh: 中文里，见面说「你好」。「你」是你，「好」是好。两个字在一起就是「希望你好」的意思。很多语言都有这样的话。说一句「你好」很简单，但是意思很大。
text_pinyin: Zhōngwén lǐ, jiànmiàn shuō「nǐ hǎo」.「Nǐ」shì nǐ,「hǎo」shì hǎo. Liǎng gè zì zài yìqǐ jiùshì「xīwàng nǐ hǎo」de yìsi. Hěn duō yǔyán dōu yǒu zhèyàng de huà. Shuō yí jù「nǐ hǎo」hěn jiǎndān, dànshì yìsi hěn dà.
text_en: In Chinese, you say 'ni hao' when you meet. 'Ni' means you, 'hao' means good. Together they mean 'I hope you are well.' Many languages have this kind of expression. Saying 'ni hao' is simple, but the meaning is big.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: Ni hao means 'I hope you are well' — the greeting itself is about wishing the other person well.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: The passage explains that saying ni hao carries the meaning of hoping someone is well — it's about mutual care.

questions.q_zh: 其他语言也有这样的话吗？
questions.q_en: Do other languages have this kind of expression?
questions.explanation: Look for 很多语言都有 (many languages have it) in the passage — many languages have this kind of expression.

title: How a Clock Works
text_zh: 钟有两个针。长的针走得快，是分针。短的针走得慢，是时针。长的针走一圈是一个小时。短的针走一圈是十二个小时。所以看钟的时候，先看短的，再看长的。
text_pinyin: Zhōng yǒu liǎng gè zhēn. Cháng de zhēn zǒu de kuài, shì fēn zhēn. Duǎn de zhēn zǒu de màn, shì shí zhēn. Cháng de zhēn zǒu yì quān shì yí gè xiǎoshí. Duǎn de zhēn zǒu yì quān shì shí èr gè xiǎoshí. Suǒyǐ kàn zhōng de shíhou, xiān kàn duǎn de, zài kàn cháng de.
text_en: A clock has two hands. The long one moves fast — it's the minute hand. The short one moves slow — it's the hour hand. The long hand going around once is one hour. The short hand going around once is twelve hours. So when reading a clock, first look at the short one, then the long one.

questions.q_zh: 安静的场景传达了什么信息？
questions.q_en: What does the quiet scene communicate?
questions.explanation: A clock's hands moving steadily and predictably — the orderly description creates a feeling of calm regularity.

questions.q_zh: 长的针走一圈是多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long does one rotation of the long hand take?
questions.explanation: Look for 一个小时 (one hour) in the passage — the long hand going around once is one hour.

questions.q_zh: 安静的场景传达了什么信息？
questions.q_en: What does the quiet scene communicate?
questions.explanation: The steady, methodical description of clock hands creates a sense of quiet order.

title: Why Tea Gets Cold
text_zh: 刚泡的茶很热。过了十分钟，茶就不太热了。过了半个小时，茶就冷了。为什么？因为热的东西会把热给旁边冷的空气。所以要喝热茶，就要快一点喝。
text_pinyin: Gāng pào de chá hěn rè. Guò le shí fēnzhōng, chá jiù bú tài rè le. Guò le bàn ge xiǎoshí, chá jiù lěng le. Wèishénme? Yīnwèi rè de dōngxi huì bǎ rè gěi pángbiān lěng de kōngqì. Suǒyǐ yào hē rè chá, jiù yào kuài yìdiǎn hē.
text_en: Freshly brewed tea is hot. After ten minutes, it's not so hot. After half an hour, it's cold. Why? Because hot things give their heat to the cold air around them. So if you want hot tea, you need to drink it quickly.

questions.q_zh: 过了多长时间茶就冷了？
questions.q_en: How long until the tea gets cold?
questions.explanation: Look for 半个小时 (half an hour) in the third sentence — after half an hour, the tea is cold.

questions.q_en: Why does tea get cold?

options.text: 热的东西把热给冷的空气
options.pinyin: rè de dōngxi bǎ rè gěi lěng de kōngqì
options.text_en: hot things give heat to cold air

options.pinyin: yǒu rén jiā le lěng shuǐ

questions.explanation: The key is 把热给旁边冷, which tells us hot things give their heat to the cold air around them.

questions.q_en: What should you do if you want hot tea?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 所以要喝热茶 — if you want hot tea, you need to drink it quickly.

title: Left and Right
text_zh: 小的时候，我不知道哪边是左，哪边是右。妈妈教我一个办法——用筷子的手是右手。这样我就记住了。但是有的人用左手吃饭。那他们怎么办呢？我想了很久，没有想到好办法。
text_pinyin: Xiǎo de shíhou, wǒ bù zhīdào nǎ biān shì zuǒ, nǎ biān shì yòu. Māma jiāo wǒ yí gè bànfǎ——yòng kuàizi de shǒu shì yòu shǒu. Zhèyàng wǒ jiù jì zhù le. Dànshì yǒu de rén yòng zuǒ shǒu chī fàn. Nà tāmen zěnme bàn ne? Wǒ xiǎng le hěn jiǔ, méiyǒu xiǎng dào hǎo bànfǎ.
text_en: When I was small, I didn't know which side was left and which was right. Mom taught me a trick — the hand you hold chopsticks with is the right hand. That's how I remembered. But some people eat with their left hand. What do they do? I thought about it for a long time but couldn't think of a good solution.

questions.q_en: What was mom's method?

options.pinyin: yòng kuàizi de shǒu shì yòu shǒu
options.text_en: the chopstick hand is the right hand

options.pinyin: xiě zì de shǒu shì zuǒ shǒu
options.text_en: the writing hand is the left hand

questions.explanation: Look for 用筷子的手 (the chopstick hand is the right hand) in the second sentence — mom's trick is: the hand you hold chopsticks with.

questions.q_en: What's the problem with this method?

options.pinyin: yǒu rén yòng zuǒ shǒu chī fàn
options.text_en: some people eat with left hand

options.pinyin: bú shì měi tiān chī fàn

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 用左手吃饭 — some people eat with their left hand, which breaks the trick.

questions.q_en: Did 'I' think of a good solution?
questions.explanation: Look for 没有想到好办 — it directly confirms no.

title: The Rice Cooker
text_zh: 我的电饭锅每天早上六点自己开始做饭。我设好了时间。这样我起来的时候，米饭已经好了。我觉得这是我买过的最好的东西。每天早上有热米饭，一天都会好。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de diànfànguō měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn zìjǐ kāishǐ zuò fàn. Wǒ shè hǎo le shíjiān. Zhèyàng wǒ qǐlái de shíhou, mǐfàn yǐjīng hǎo le. Wǒ juéde zhè shì wǒ mǎi guò de zuì hǎo de dōngxi. Měi tiān zǎoshang yǒu rè mǐfàn, yì tiān dōu huì hǎo.
text_en: My rice cooker starts cooking on its own every morning at six. I set the timer. So when I wake up, the rice is already done. I think it's the best thing I've ever bought. With hot rice every morning, the whole day will be good.

questions.q_en: What time does the rice cooker start?
questions.explanation: Look for 电饭锅每天早 (six) in the opening line — the rice cooker starts cooking at six.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: Setting a timer every day for hot morning rice — this daily ritual clearly matters a lot to the narrator.

questions.q_zh: 为什么「我」觉得一天都会好？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think the whole day will be good?
questions.explanation: The key is 电饭锅每天早, which tells us with hot rice every morning, the whole day will be good.

title: The Breakfast Argument
text_zh: 每天早上，我和我太太都要想吃什么。我想吃面包，她想吃粥。我说「吃面包吧」，她说「吃粥吧」。最后我们总是去楼下的饭馆——她吃粥，我吃面包。两个人都高兴。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang, wǒ hé wǒ tàitai dōu yào xiǎng chī shénme. Wǒ xiǎng chī miànbāo, tā xiǎng chī zhōu. Wǒ shuō「chī miànbāo ba」, tā shuō「chī zhōu ba」. Zuìhòu wǒmen zǒngshì qù lóu xià de fànguǎn——tā chī zhōu, wǒ chī miànbāo. Liǎng gè rén dōu gāoxìng.
text_en: Every morning, my wife and I have to decide what to eat. I want bread, she wants porridge. I say 'Let's eat bread,' she says 'Let's eat porridge.' In the end we always go to the restaurant downstairs — she eats porridge, I eat bread. Both of us are happy.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: They disagree every morning but both get what they want at the restaurant — the result is contentment for both.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: The playful daily disagreement that always resolves happily — the author wants you to feel that gentle satisfaction.

questions.q_en: Does this happen every day?
questions.explanation: Look for 总是一样 (always the same) — in the end we always go.

title: The Cold Noodles in Summer
text_zh: 夏天太热了，我不想吃热的东西。我去买了凉面。面条是冷的，上面有菜。我坐在店里吃。外面很热，里面有空调。吃完凉面，我觉得好多了。夏天就应该吃凉面。
text_pinyin: Xiàtiān tài rè le, wǒ bù xiǎng chī rè de dōngxi. Wǒ qù mǎi le liángmiàn. Miàntiáo shì lěng de, shàngmiàn yǒu cài. Wǒ zuò zài diàn lǐ chī. Wàimiàn hěn rè, lǐmiàn yǒu kōngtiáo. Chī wán liángmiàn, wǒ juéde hǎo duō le. Xiàtiān jiù yīnggāi chī liángmiàn.
text_en: Summer is too hot — I don't want to eat hot food. I went and bought cold noodles. The noodles are cold, with vegetables on top. I sat in the shop and ate. Outside it's hot, inside there's air conditioning. After eating cold noodles, I felt much better. Summer should be for cold noodles.

questions.q_en: Why doesn't 'I' want hot food?
questions.explanation: The key is 夏天太热了，, which tells us summer is too hot, so the narrator doesn't want hot food.

questions.q_en: What's on top of the cold noodles?
questions.explanation: Look for 菜 (vegetables) in the third sentence — the cold noodles have vegetables on top.

questions.q_zh: 吃完以后「我」觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after eating?
questions.explanation: The clue is 好多了 (much better) in the passage — after eating cold noodles,.

title: Cooking for One
text_zh: 一个人住，一个人做饭。做多了吃不完，做少了不够。今天我做了一碗面条，刚好吃完。我很高兴。但是锅碗还是要洗。一个人做饭，一个人洗碗。这是一个人住的样子。
text_pinyin: Yí gè rén zhù, yí gè rén zuò fàn. Zuò duō le chī bù wán, zuò shǎo le bú gòu. Jīntiān wǒ zuò le yì wǎn miàntiáo, gānghǎo chī wán. Wǒ hěn gāoxìng. Dànshì guō wǎn háishi yào xǐ. Yí gè rén zuò fàn, yí gè rén xǐ wǎn. Zhè shì yí gè rén zhù de yàngzi.
text_en: Living alone, cooking alone. Make too much and you can't finish, make too little and it's not enough. Today I made a bowl of noodles — just right, finished it all. I was happy. But the pot and bowl still need washing. Cook alone, wash alone. That's what living alone looks like.

questions.q_en: What did 'I' make today?
questions.explanation: Look for 面条 (noodles) in the third sentence — today I made a bowl of noodles.

questions.q_en: How was the portion?
questions.explanation: Look for 做了一碗面条 (just right) in the third sentence — just right, finished it all.

questions.q_en: Who does 'I' live with?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 一个人 — living alone, cooking alone, washing alone — the narrator lives by themselves.

title: The Old Man's Walk
text_zh: 每天早上六点，一个老人从我家门口走过。他走得很慢。他的手里有一个收音机，放着音乐。他不看手机，不看别人，就是走。七点的时候，他会走回来。每天都一样。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn, yí gè lǎorén cóng wǒ jiā ménkǒu zǒu guò. Tā zǒu de hěn màn. Tā de shǒu lǐ yǒu yí gè shōuyīnjī, fàngzhe yīnyuè. Tā bú kàn shǒujī, bú kàn biérén, jiùshì zǒu. Qī diǎn de shíhou, tā huì zǒu huílái. Měi tiān dōu yíyàng.
text_en: Every morning at six, an old man walks past my door. He walks very slowly. He has a radio in his hand, playing music. He doesn't look at a phone, doesn't look at anyone — just walks. At seven, he walks back. Every day the same.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: He walks with his radio at the same time every day — breaking that routine would feel wrong.

questions.q_en: How long does he walk?
questions.explanation: He leaves at six and returns at seven — that's one hour of walking.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: The same walk, same time, every day — disrupting it would leave him feeling unsettled.

title: The Light in the Kitchen
text_zh: 晚上十二点了，大家都睡了。但是厨房的灯还开着。我走过去看——妈妈在厨房里，一个人坐着喝茶。她看到我说「你怎么没睡？」我说「你也没睡啊。」她笑了，说「有时候我喜欢一个人安静地坐一会儿。」
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shí èr diǎn le, dàjiā dōu shuì le. Dànshì chúfáng de dēng hái kāizhe. Wǒ zǒu guòqù kàn——māma zài chúfáng lǐ, yí gè rén zuòzhe hē chá. Tā kàn dào wǒ shuō「nǐ zěnme méi shuì?」Wǒ shuō「nǐ yě méi shuì a.」Tā xiào le, shuō「yǒu shíhou wǒ xǐhuan yí gè rén ānjìng de zuò yíhuìr.」
text_en: It's midnight, everyone is asleep. But the kitchen light is still on. I went to look — mom is in the kitchen, sitting alone drinking tea. She saw me and said 'Why aren't you asleep?' I said 'You're not asleep either.' She laughed and said 'Sometimes I like to sit quietly by myself for a while.'

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: Mom sitting quietly drinking tea at midnight, sharing a gentle laugh with her child — the tone is warm and loving.

questions.q_en: What time is it?
questions.explanation: Look for 十二点 (twelve) in the opening line — it's midnight — twelve o'clock.

questions.q_en: Why isn't mom asleep?

options.pinyin: xǐhuan yí gè rén ānjìng de zuò

questions.explanation: Mom says sometimes she likes to sit quietly by herself for a while.

title: The Shoes at the Door
text_zh: 从门口的鞋，你可以知道很多事。大的鞋是爸爸的。小的鞋是女儿的。运动鞋是儿子的。红的鞋是妈妈的。今天门口只有一双鞋——爸爸的。家里其他人都出去了。他一个人在家。
text_pinyin: Cóng ménkǒu de xié, nǐ kěyǐ zhīdào hěn duō shì. Dà de xié shì bàba de. Xiǎo de xié shì nǚér de. Yùndòngxié shì érzi de. Hóng de xié shì māma de. Jīntiān ménkǒu zhǐ yǒu yì shuāng xié——bàba de. Jiā lǐ qítā rén dōu chūqù le. Tā yí gè rén zài jiā.
text_en: From the shoes at the door, you can know a lot. The big shoes are dad's. The small shoes are the daughter's. The sports shoes are the son's. The red shoes are mom's. Today there's only one pair at the door — dad's. Everyone else went out. He's home alone.

questions.q_en: Whose are the red shoes?
questions.explanation: Look for 妈妈 (mom's) in the middle of the passage — the red shoes are mom's.

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: Only dad's shoes remain at the door — the image of a once-full entryway now empty stirs feelings tied to memory.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: Everyone's shoes are gone except dad's — he's alone, and the empty doorway hints at absence and longing.
